 LIONEL . - " Monster ! hold your tongue . a propos of marriage , why be you 
 still single ? " 

 VANCE . - " because I have no wish to be double up ! moreover , man be like 
 a napkin , the more neatly the housewife double he , the more carefully 
 she lay he on the shelf . neither can a man once double know how often 
 he may be double . not only his wife fold he in two , but every child 
 quarter he into a new double , till what be a wide and handsome 
 substance , large enough for anything in reason , dwindle into a pitiful 
 square that will not cover one platter , - all pucker and crease , 
 small and small with every double , with every double a new crease . 
 then , my friend , come the washing - bill ! and , besides all the hurt 
 one receive in the mangle , consider the hourly wear and tear of the 
 linen - press ! in short , Shakspeare vindicate the single life , and 
 depict the double in the famous line , which be no doubt intend to be 
 allegorical of marriage , 

  " ' double , double , toil and trouble . ' 

 besides , no single man can be fairly call poor . what double man can 
 with certainty be call rich ? a single man can lodge in a garret , and 
 dine on a herring : nobody know ; nobody care . let he marry , and he 
 invite the world to witness where he lodge , and how he din . the 
 first necessary a wife demand be the most ruinous , the most indefinite 
 superfluity ; it be gentility accord to what her neighbour call 
 genteel . gentility commence with the honeymoon ; it be its shadow , and 
 lengthen as the moon decline . when the honey be all go , your bride 
 say , ' we can have our tea without sugar when quite alone , love ; but , 
 in case gentility drop in , here be a bill for silver sugar - tong ! ' that be 
 why I be single . " 

 " economy again , Vance . " 

 " Prudence , - dignity , " answer Vance , seriously ; and sink into a 
 revery that seem gloomy , he shoot back to shore . 




 CHAPTER II . 

  Mr. Vance explain how he come to grind colour and save half - pence . 
  - a sudden announcement . 

 the meal be over ; the table have be spread by a window that look 
 upon the river . the moon be up : the young man ask for no other 
 light ; conversation between they - often shift , often pause - have 
 gradually become grave , as it usually do with two companion in youth ; 
 while yet long vista in the Future stretch before they deep in shadow , 
 and they fall into confide talk on what they wish , - what they fear ; 
 make visionary map in that limitless Obscure . 

 " there be so much power in faith , " say Lionel , " even when faith be 
 apply but to thing human and earthly , that let a man be but firmly 
 persuade that he be bear to do , some day , what at the moment seem 
 impossible , and it be fifty to one but what he do it before he die . 
 surely , when you be a child at school , you feel convinced that there 
 be something in your fate distinct from that of the other boy , whom 
 the master might call quite as clever , - feel that faith in yourself 
 which make you sure that you would be one day what you be . " 

 " well , I suppose so ; but vague aspiration and self - conceit must be 
 bind together by some practical necessity - perhaps a very homely and 
 a very vulgar one - or they scatter and evaporate . one would think that 
 rich people in high life ought to do more than poor folk in humble 
 life . More pain be take with their education ; they have more leisure 
 for follow the bent of their genius : yet it be the poor folk , often 
 half self - educate , and with pinched belly , that do three - fourth of 
 the world 's grand labour . poverty be the keen stimulant ; and poverty 
 make I say , not ' I will do , ' but ' I must . ' " 

 " you know real poverty in childhood , Frank ? " 

 " real poverty , cover over with sham affluence . my father be Genteel 
 Poverty , and my mother be poor gentility . the sham affluence go when 
 my father die . the real poverty then come out in all its ugliness . I 
 be take from a genteel school , at which , long afterwards , I 
 genteelly pay the bill ; and I have to support my mother somehow or 
 other , - somehow or other I succeed . alas , I fear not genteelly ! but 
 before I lose she , which I do in a few year , she have some comfort 
 which be not appearance ; and she kindly allow , dear soul , that 
 gentility and sham do not go well together . oh , beware of debt , 
 Lionello mio ; and never call that economy meanness which be but the 
 safeguard from mean degradation . " 

 " I understand you at last , Vance ; shake hand : I know why you be 
 save . " 

 " habit now , " answer Vance , repress praise of himself , as usual . 
 " but I remember so well when twopence be a sum to be respect that 
 to this day I would rather put it by than spend it . all our idea - like 
 orange - plant - spread out in proportion to the size of the box which 
 imprison the root . then I have a sister . " Vance pause a moment , as 
 if in pain , but go on with seem carelessness , lean over the 
 window - sill , and turn his face from his friend . " I have a sister old 
 than myself , handsome , gentle . " 

 " I be so proud of she ! foolish girl ! my love be not enough for she . 
 foolish girl ! she could not wait to see what I might live to do for she . 
 she marry - oh ! so genteelly ! - a young man , very well bear , who have 
 woo she before my father die . he have the villany to remain constant 
 when she have not a farthing , and he be dependent on distant relation , 
 and his own domain in Parnassus . the wretch be a poet ! so they 
 marry . they spend their honeymoon genteelly , I dare say . his relation 
 cut he . Parnassus pay no rent . he go abroad . such heart - rend 
 letter from she . they be destitute . how I work ! how I rag ! but 
 how could I maintain she and her husband too , mere child that I be ? no 
 matter . they be dead now , both ; all dead for whose sake I first ground 
 colour and save halfpence . and Frank Vance be a stingy , selfish 
 bachelor . never revive this dull subject again , or I shall borrow a 
 crown from you and cut you dead . Waiter , ho ! - the bill . I will just go 
 round to the stable , and see the horse put to . " 

 as the friend re - enter London , Vance say , " set I down anywhere in 
 Piccadilly ; I will walk home . you , I suppose , of course , be stay 
 with your mother in Gloucester Place ? " 

 " no , " say Lionel , rather embarrassed ; " Colonel Morley , who act for I 
 as if he be my guardian , take a lodging for I in Chesterfield Street , 
 Mayfair . my hour , I fear , would ill suit my dear mother . only in 
 town two day ; and , thank to Morley , my table be already cover with 
 invitation . " 

 " yet you give I one day , generous friend ! " 

 " you the second day , my mother the first . but there be three ball 
 before I to - night . come home with I , and smoke your cigar while I 
 dress . " 

 " no ; but I will at least light my cigar in your hall , prodigal ! " 

 Lionel now stop at his lodging . the groom , who serve he also as 
 valet , be in wait at the door . " a note for you , sir , from Colonel 
 Morley , - just come . " Lionel hastily open it , and read , 

  MY DEAR HAUGHTON , - Mr. Darrell have suddenly arrive in London . keep 
  yourself free all to - morrow , when , no doubt , he will see you . I be 
  hurry off to he . 

  your in haste , A. V. M. 




 CHAPTER III . 

  once more Guy Darrell . 

 Guy Darrell be alone : a lofty room in a large house on the first 
 floor , - his own house in Carlton Gardens , which he have occupy 
 during his brief and brilliant parliamentary career ; since then , leave 
 contemptuously to the care of a house agent , to be let by year or by 
 season , it have know various tenant of an opulence and station suitable 
 to its space and site . dinner and concert , rout and ball , have 
 assemble the friend and jade the spirit of many a gracious host 
 and smile hostess . the tenure of one of these temporary occupant have 
 recently expire ; and , ere the agent have find another , the long absent 
 owner drop down into its silence hall as from the cloud , without 
 other establishment than his old servant Mills and the woman in charge 
 of the house . there , as in a caravansery , the traveller take his rest , 
 stately and desolate . nothing so comfortless as one of those large 
 London house all to one 's self . in long row against the wall stand 
 the empty fauteuil . spectral from the gild ceiling hang lightless 
 chandelier . - the furniture , pompous , but wear by use and fade by time , 
 seem memento of depart revel . when you return to your house in 
 the country - no matter how long the absence , no matter how decay by 
 neglect the friendly chamber may be , if it have only be desert 
 in the meanwhile   not let to new race , who , by their own shifting 
 dynasty , have supplant the rightful lord , and half - efface his 
 memorial   - the wall may still greet you forgivingly , the character of 
 Home be still there . you take up again the thread of association which 
 have , be suspend , not snap . but it be otherwise with a house in 
 city , especially in our fast - live London , where few house descend 
 from father to son , - where the title - deed be rarely more than those of 
 a purchase lease for a term of year , after which your property quit 
 you . a house in London , which your father never enter , in which no 
 elbow - chair , no old - fashioned work - table , recall to you the kind smile 
 of a mother ; a house that you have leave as you leave an inn , let to 
 people whose name you scarce know , with as little respect for your 
 family record as you have for theirs , - when you return after a long 
 interval of year to a house like that , you stand , as stand Darrell , a 
 forlorn stranger under your own roof - tree . what care he for those who 
 have last gather round those hearth with their chill steely grate , 
 whose form have recline on those formal couch , whose foot have wear 
 away the gloss from those costly carpet ? history in the life of many 
 might be record within those wall . " lover there have breathe their 
 first vow ; bridal feast have be hold ; babe have crow in the arm of 
 proud young mother ; politician there have be raise into minister ; 
 minister there have fall back into independent member ; " through those 
 door corps have be bear forth to relentless vault . for these race 
 and their record what care the owner ? their writing be not on the 
 wall . sponge out , as from a slate , their reckoning with Time ; leave 
 dim , here and there , some chance scratch of his own , blurred and bygone . 
 lean against the mantelpiece , Darrell gaze round the room with a 
 vague wistful look , as if seek to conjure up association that 
 might link the present hour to that past life which have slip away 
 elsewhere ; and his profile , reflect on the mirror behind , pale and 
 mournful , seem like that ghost of himself which his memory silently 
 evoke . 

 the man be but little alter externally since we see he last , however 
 inly change since he last stand on those unwelcoming floor ; the form 
 still retain the same vigour and symmetry , - the same unspeakable 
 dignity of mien and bearing ; the same thoughtful bend of the proud 
 neck , - so distinct , in its elastic rebound , from the stoop of debility 
 or age , thick as ever the rich mass of dark - brown hair , though , when in 
 the impatience of some painful thought his hand sweep the loose curl 
 from his forehead , the silver thread might now be see shoot here 
 and there , - vanish almost as soon as see . no , whatever the baptismal 
 register may say to the contrary , that man be not old , - not even 
 elderly ; in the deep of that clear gray eye light may be calm , but 
 in calm it be vivid ; not a ray , send from brain or from heart , be yet 
 flicker down . on the whole , however , there be less composure than of 
 old in his mien and bearing ; less of that resignation which seem to 
 say , " I have do with the substance of life . " still there be gloom , 
 but it be more broken and restless . evidently that human breast be 
 again admit , or force itself to court , human hope , human object . 
 return to the substance of life , their movement be see in the 
 shadow which , when they wrap we round at remoter distance , seem to 
 lose their trouble as they gain their width . he break from his musing 
 attitude with an abrupt angry movement , as if shake off thought 
 which displease he , and gather his arm tightly to his breast , in 
 a gesture peculiar to himself , walk to and fro the room , murmur 
 inaudibly . the door open ; he turn quickly , and with an evident sense 
 of relief , for his face brighten . " Alban , my dear Alban ! " 

 " Darrell ! old friend ! old school - friend ! dear , dear Guy Darrell ! " the 
 two Englishmen stand , hand tightly clasped in each other , in true 
 english greeting , their eye moisten with remembrance that carry 
 they back to boyhood . 

 Alban be the first to recover self - possession ; and , when the friend 
 have seat themselves , he survey Darrell 's countenance deliberately , 
 and say , " so little change ! - wonderful ! what be your secret ? " 

 " suspense from life , - hibernating . but you beat I ; you have be 
 spend life , yet seem as rich in it as when we part . " 

 " no ; I begin to decry the present and laud the past ; to read with 
 glass , to decide from prejudice , to recoil from change , to find sense 
 in twaddle , to know the value of health from the fear to lose it ; to 
 feel an interest in rheumatism , an awe of bronchitis ; to tell anecdote , 
 and to wear flannel . to you in strict confidence I disclose the truth : I 
 be no long twenty - five . you laugh ; this be civilized talk : do it not 
 refresh you after the gibberish you must have chatter in Asia Minor ? " 

 Darrell might have answer in the affirmative with truth . what man , 
 after long year of solitude , be not refresh by talk , however trivial , 
 that recall to he the gay time of the world he remember in his 
 young day , - and recall it to he on the lip of a friend in youth ! but 
 Darrell say nothing ; only he settle himself in his chair with a 
 more cheerful ease , and incline his relax brow with a nod of 
 encouragement or assent . 

 Colonel Morley continue . " but when do you arrive ? whence ? how long do 
 you stay here ? what be your plan ? " 

 DARRELL . - " Caesar could not be more laconic . when arrive ? this evening . 
 whence ? Ouzelford . how long do I stay ? uncertain . what be my plan ? let 
 we discuss they . " 

 COLONEL MORLEY . - " with all my heart . you have plan , then ? - a good sign . 
 animal in hibernation form none . " 

 DARRELL   put aside the light on the table , so as to leave , his face 
 in shade , and look towards the floor as he speak . - " for the last 
 five year I have struggle hard to renew interest in mankind , reconnect 
 myself with common life and its healthful object . between Fawley 
 and London I desire to form a magnetic medium . I take rather a vast 
 one , - nearly all the rest of the known world . I have visit both 
 Americas , either end . all Asia have I ransack , and pierce as far 
 into Africa as traveller ever go in search of timbuctoo . but I have 
 sojourn also , at long interval , at least they seem long to I , - in 
 the gay capital of Europe   Paris except ; mixed , too , with the 
 gayest ; hire palace , fill they with guest ; feast and hear music . 
 ' Guy Darrell , ' say I , ' shake off the rust of year : thou hadst no youth 
 while young , - be young now . a holiday may restore thee to wholesome 
 work , as a holiday restore the weary school - boy . ' " 

 COLONEL MORLEY . - " I comprehend ; the experiment succeed ? " 

 DARRELL . - " I do not   know : not yet ; but it may . I be here , and I intend 
 to stay . I would not go to a hotel for a single day , lest my resolution 
 should fail I . I have throw myself into this castle of care without 
 even a garrison . I hope to hold it . help I to man it . in a word , and 
 without metaphor , I be here with the design of re - enter London life . " 

 COLONEL MORLEY . - " I be so glad . hearty congratulation ! how rejoice 
 all the Viponts will be ! another ' CRISIS ' be at hand . you have see the 
 newspaper regularly , of course : the state of the country interest you . 
 you say that you come from Ouzelford , the town you once represent . I 
 guess you will re - enter Parliament ; you have but to say the word . " 

 DARRELL . - " Parliament ! no . I receive , while abroad , so earnest a 
 request from my old constituent to lay the foundation - stone of a new 
 Town - Hall , in which they be much interested ; and my obligation to they 
 have be so great that I could not refuse . I write to fix the day as 
 soon as I have resolve to return to England , make a condition that I 
 should be spar the infliction of a public dinner , and land just in 
 time to keep my appointment ; reach Ouzelford early this morning , go 
 through the ceremony , make a short speech , come on at once to London , 
 not venture to diverge to Fawley   which be not very far from 
 Ouzelford , lest , once there again , I should not have strength to leave 
 it ; and here I be . " Darrell pause , then repeat , in brisk emphatic 
 tone , " Parliament ? no . labour ? no . fellow - man , I be about to confess 
 to you : I would snatch back some day of youth , - a wintry likeness of 
 youth , well than none . old friend , let we amuse ourselves ! when I 
 be work hard , hard , hard ! it be you who would say : ' come forth , be 
 amuse , ' - you ! happy butterfly that you be ! now , I say to you , ' show 
 I this flaunt town that you know so well ; initiate I into the joy 
 of polite pleasure , social commune , 

  " ' Dulce mihi furere est amico . " 

 you have amusement , - let I share they . ' " 

 " faith , " quoth the Colonel , cross his leg , " you come late in the 
 day ! amusement cease to amuse at last . I have try all , and begin to 
 be tired . I have have my holiday , exhaust its sport ; and you , come 
 from book and desk fresh into the playground , say , ' football and 
 leapfrog . ' alas ! my poor friend , why do not you come soon ? " 

 DARRELL . - " one word , one question . you have make EASE a philosophy and a 
 system ; no man ever do so with more felicitous grace : nor , in follow 
 pleasure , have you part company with conscience and shame . a fine 
 gentleman ever , in honour as in elegance . well , be you satisfied with 
 your choice of life ? be you happy ? " 

 " Happy ! who be ? satisfy , perhaps . " 

 " be there any one you envy , - whose choice , other than your own , you 
 would prefer ? " 

 " certainly . " 

 " who ? " 

 " you . " 

 " I ! " say Darrell , open his eye with unaffected amaze . " I ! envy I ! 
 prefer my choice ! " 

 COLONEL MORLEY   peevishly . - " without doubt . you have have gratify 
 ambition , a great career . envy you ! who would not ? your own object in 
 life fulfil : you covet distinction , - you win it ; fortune , - your 
 wealth be immense ; the restoration of your name and lineage from 
 obscurity and humiliation , - be not name and lineage again write in 
 the Libro d'oro ? what king would not hail you as his counsellor ? what 
 senate not open its rank to admit you as a chief ? what house , though 
 the haughty in the land , would not accept your alliance ? and withal , 
 you stand before I stalwart and unbowe , young blood still in your 
 vein . ungrateful man , who would not change lot with Guy Darrell ? fame , 
 fortune , health , and , not to flatter you , a form and presence that 
 would be remark , though you stand in that black frock by the side of a 
 monarch in his coronation robe . " 

 DARRELL . - " you have turn my question against myself with a kindliness 
 of intention that make I forgive your belief in my vanity . pass 
 on , - or rather pass back ; you say you have try all in life that 
 distract or sweeten . not so , lone bachelor ; you have not try 
 wedlock . have not that be your mistake ? " 

 COLONEL MORLEY . - " answer for yourself . you have try it . " the word 
 be scarce out of his mouth ere he repent the retort ; for Darrell 
 start as if sting to the quick ; and his brow , before serene , his 
 lip , before playful , grow , the one darkly trouble , the other tightly 
 compress . " pardon I , " falter out the friend . 

 DARRELL . - " oh , yes ! I bring it on myself . what stuff we have be 
 talk ! tell I the news , not political , any other . but first , your 
 report of young Haughton . cordial thank for all your kindness to he . 
 you write I word that he be much improve , - most likeable ; you add , 
 that at Paris he become the rage , that in London you be sure he will 
 be extremely popular . be it so , if for his own sake . be you quite sure 
 that it be not for the expectation which I come here to disperse ? " 

 COLONEL MORLEY . - " much for himself , I be certain ; a little , perhaps , 
 because - whatever he think , and I say to the contrary - people see no 
 other heir to your property - " 

 " I understand , " interrupt Darrell , quickly . " but he do not nurse 
 those expectation ? he will not be disappoint ? " 

 COLONEL MORLEY . - " verily I believe that , apart from his love for you and 
 a delicacy of sentiment that would recoil from plant hope of wealth 
 in the graf of benefactor , Lionel Haughton would prefer carve his 
 own fortune to all the ingot hew out of California by another 's hand 
 and bequeath by another 's will . " 

 DARRELL . - " I be heartily glad to hear and to trust you . " 

 COLONEL MORLEY . - " I gather from what you say that you be here with the 
 intention to - to - " 

 " Marry again , " say Darrell , firmly . " right . I be . " 

 " I always feel sure you would marry again . be the lady here too ? " 

 " what lady ? " 

 " the lady you have choose . " 

 " Tush ! I have choose none . I come here to choose ; and in this I 
 ask advice from your experience . I would marry again ! I ! at my age ! 
 ridiculous ! but so it be . you know all the mother and marriageable 
 daughter that London - arida nutrix - rear for nuptial altar : where , 
 amongst they , shall I , Guy Darrell , the man whom you think so enviable , 
 find the safe helpmate , whose love he may reward with munificent 
 jointure , to whose child he may bequeath the name that have now no 
 successor , and the wealth he have no heart to spend ? " 

 Colonel Morley - who , as we know , be by habit a matchmaker , and like the 
 vocation - assume a placid but cogitative mien , rub his brow gently , 
 and say in his soft , well - breed accent , " you would not marry a mere 
 girl ? some one of suitable age . I know several most superior young 
 woman on the other side of thirty , Wilhelmina Prymme , for instance , or 
 Janet - " 

 DARRELL . - " old maid . no ! decidedly no ! " 

 COLONEL MORLEY   suspiciously . - " but you would not risk the peace 
 of your old age with a girl of eighteen , or else I do know a very 
 accomplished , well - bring - up girl ; just eighteen , who - " 

 DARRELL . - " re - enter life by the side of Eighteen ! be I a madman ? " 

 COLONEL MORLEY . - " neither old maid nor young maid ; the choice become 
 narrow . you would prefer a widow . ha ! I have think of one ; a prize , 
 indeed , could you but win she , the widow of - " 

 DARRELL . - " Ephesus ! - bah ! suggest no widow to I . a widow , with her 
 affection bury in the grave ! " 

 MORLEY . - " not necessarily . and in this case - " 

 DARRELL   interrupting , and with warmth . - " in every case I tell you : no 
 widow shall doff her weed for I . do she love the first man ? Fickle 
 be the woman who can love twice . do she not love he ? why do she marry 
 he ? perhaps she sell herself to a rent - roll ? shall she sell herself 
 again to I for a jointure ? Heaven forbid ! talk not of widow . no dainty 
 so flavourless as a heart warm up again . " 

 COLONEL MORLEY . - " neither maid , be they old or young , nor widow . 
 possibly you want an angel . London be not the place for angel . " 

 DARRELL . - " I grant that the choice seem involve in perplexity . how 
 can it be otherwise if one 's self be perplexed ? and yet , Alban , I 
 be serious ; and I do not presume to be so exact as my word have 
 imply . I ask not fortune , nor rank beyond gentle blood , nor youth nor 
 beauty nor accomplishment nor fashion , but I do ask one thing , and one 
 thing only . " 

 COLONEL MORLEY . - " what be that ? you have leave nothing worth the having 
 to ask for . " 

 DARRELL . - " nothing ! I have leave all ! I ask some one whom I can love ; 
 love well than all the world , - not the mariage de convenance , not 
 the mariage de raison , but the mariage d'amour . all other marriage , 
 with vow of love so solemn , with intimacy of commune so close , - all 
 other marriage , in my eye , be an act falsehood , a varnish sin . ah , 
 if I have think so always ! but away regret and repentance ! the future 
 alone be now before I ! Alban Morley ! I would sign away all I have in 
 the world   save the old house at Fawley , ay , and after signing , cut 
 off to boot this right hand , could I but once fall in love ; love , and 
 be love again , as any two of Heaven 's simple human creature may 
 love each other while life be fresh ! strange ! strange ! look out into 
 the world ; mark the man of our year who shall be most court , most 
 adulate , or admire . give he all the attribute of power , wealth , 
 royalty , genius , fame . see all the young generation bow before he 
 with hope or awe : his word can make their fortune ; at his smile a 
 reputation dawn . well ; now let that man say to the young , ' room amongst 
 yourself : all that win I this homage I would lay at the foot of 
 Beauty . I enter the list of love , ' and straightway his power vanishe , 
 the poor booby of twenty - four can jostle he aside ; before , the 
 object of reverence , he be now the butt of ridicule . the instant he ask 
 right to win the heart of a woman , a boy whom in all else he could rule 
 as a lackey cry , ' Off , Graybeard , that realm at least be mine ! ' " 

 COLONEL MORLEY . - " this be but eloquent extravagance , even if your 
 beard be gray . man old than you , and with half your pretension , 
 even of outward form , have carry away heart from boy like Adonis . 
 only choose well : that be the difficulty ; if it be not difficult , who 
 would be a bachelor ? " 

 DARRELL . - " guide my choice . pilot I to the haven . " 

 COLONEL MORLEY . - " Accepted ! but you must remount a suitable 
 establishment ; reopen your way to the great world , and penetrate those 
 sacred recess where await spinster weave the fatal web . leave all 
 to I . let Mills   I see you have he still   call on I to - morrow about 
 your menage . you will give dinner , of course ? " 

 DARRELL . - " oh , of course ; must I dine at they myself ? " 

 Morley laugh softly , and take up his hat . 

 " so soon ! " cry Darrell . " if I fatigue you already , what chance shall I 
 have with new friend ? " 

 " so soon ! it be past eleven . and it be you who must be fatigue . " 

 " no such good luck ; be I fatigued , I might hope to sleep . I will walk 
 back with you . leave I not alone in this room , - alone in the jaw of a 
 fish ; swallow up by a creature whose blood be cold . " 

 " you have something still to say to I , " say Alban , when they be in 
 the open air : " I detect it in your manner ; what be it ? " 

 " I know not . but you have tell I no news ; these street be grow 
 strange to I . who live now in yonder house ? once the dweller be my 
 friend . " 

 " in that house , - oh , new people ! I forget their name , - but rich ; in a 
 year or two , with luck , they may be exclusives , and forget my name . in 
 the other house , Carr Vipont still . " 

 " Vipont ; those dear vipont ! what of they all ? crawl they , ste they , 
 bask they in the sun , or be they in anxious process of a change of 
 skin ? " 

 " hush ! my dear friend : no satire on your own connection ; nothing so 
 injudicious . I be a Vipont , too , and all for the family maxim , ' vipont 
 with Vipont , and come what may ! ' " 

 " I stand rebuke . but I be no Vipont . I marry , it be true , into their 
 house , and they marry , age ago , into mine ; but no drop in the blood 
 of time - server flow through the vein of the last childless Darrell . 
 Pardon . I allow the merit of the Vipont race ; no family more excite my 
 respectful interest . what of their birth , death , and marriage ? " 

 COLONEL MORLEY . - " as to the birth , Carr have just welcome the birth of 
 a grandson ; the first - bear of his eld son   who marry last year a 
 daughter of the Duke of Halifax , - a promising young man , a Lord in the 
 Admiralty . Carr have a second son in the Hussars ; have just purchase his 
 step : the other boy be still at school . he have three daughter too , 
 fine girl , admirably bring up ; indeed , now I think of it , the eld , 
 Honoria , might suit you , highly accomplish ; well read ; interest 
 herself in politic ; a great admirer of intellect ; of a very serious 
 turn of mind too . " 

 DARRELL . - " a female politician with a serious turn of mind , - a farthe 
 rushlight in a London fog ! hasten on to subject less gloomy . whose 
 funeral achievement be that yonder ? " 

 COLONEL MORLEY . - " the late Lord Niton 's , father to Lady Montfort . " 

 DARRELL . - " Lady Montfort ! her father be a lyndsay , and die before the 
 Flood . a deluge , at least , have go over I and my world since I look 
 on the face of his widow . " 

 COLONEL MORLEY . - " I speak of the present Lord Montfort 's wife , - the 
 Earl 's . you of the poor Marquess 's , the last Marquess ; the marquisate 
 be extinct . surely , whatever your wandering , you must have hear of the 
 death of the last Marquess of Montfort ? " 

 " yes , I hear of that , " answer Darrell , in a somewhat husky and 
 muttered voice . " so he be dead , the young man ! what kill he ? " 

 COLONEL MORLEY . - " a violent attack of croup , - quite sudden . he be 
 stay at Carr 's at the time . I suspect that Carr make he talk ! a 
 thing he be not accustomed to do . derange his system altogether . but 
 do not   let we revive painful subject . " 

 DARRELL . - " be she with he at the time ? " 

 COLONEL MORLEY . - " Lady Montfort ? no ; they be very seldom together . " 

 DARRELL . - " she be not marry again yet ? " 

 COLONEL MORLEY . - " no , but still young and so beautiful she will have 
 many offer . I know those who be wait to propose . montfort have be 
 only dead eighteen month ; die just before young Carr 's marriage . 
 his widow live , in complete seclusion , at her jointure - house near 
 Twickenham . she have only see even I once since her loss . " 

 DARRELL . - " when be that ? " 

 MORLEY . - " about six or seven month ago ; she ask after you with much 
 interest . " 

 DARRELL . - " after I ! " 

 COLONEL MORLEY . - " to be sure . do not   I remember how constantly she and 
 her mother be at your house ? be it strange that she should ask after 
 you ? you ought to know she well , - the most affectionate , grateful 
 character . " 

 DARRELL . - " I dare say . but at the time you refer to , I be too occupy 
 to acquire much accurate knowledge of a young lady 's character . I should 
 have know her mother 's character well , yet I mistook even that . " 

 COLONEL MORLEY . - " Mrs. Lyndsay 's character you might well 
 mistake , - charming but artificial : Lady Montfort be natural . indeed , if

 DARRELL . - " a fashionable beauty ! and young enough to be my daughter . 
 such be human friendship ! so the marquisate be extinct , and Sir James 
 Vipont , whom I remember in the House of Commons - respectable man , 
 great authority on cattle , timid , and always say , ' do you read that 
 article in to - day 's paper ? ' - have the estate and the earldom ? " 

 COLONEL MORLEY . - " yes . there be some fear of a dispute succession , but 
 Sir James make his claim very clear . between you and I , the change have 
 be a serious affliction to the Viponts . the late lord be not wise , 
 but on state occasion he look his part , - tre grand seigneur , - and 
 Carr manage the family influence with admirable tact . the present lord 
 have the habit of a yeoman ; his wife share his taste . he have take the 
 management not only of the property , but of its influence , out of Carr 's

 obsolete politician . he will never keep the family together , impossible , 
 a sad thing . I remember how our last muster , five year ago next 
 Christmas , strike terror into Lord 's Cabinet ; the mere report of it in 
 the newspaper set all people talk and think . the result be that , 
 two week after , proper overture be make to Carr : he consent to 
 assist the minister ; and the country be save ! now , thank to this 
 stupid new earl , in eighteen month we have lose ground which it take at 
 least a century and a half to gain . our vote be divide ; our influence 
 fritter away ; Montfort House be shut up ; and Carr , grow quite thin , 
 say that in the come ' CRISIS ' a Cabinet will not only be form , but 
 will also last - last time enough for irreparable mischief - without a 
 single Vipont in office . " 

 thus Colonel Morley continue in mournful strain , Darrell silent by his 
 side , till the Colonel reach his own door . there , while apply 
 his latch - key to the lock , Alban 's mind return from the peril that 
 threaten the House of Vipont and the Star of Brunswick to the petty 
 claim of private friendship . but even these last be now blend with 
 those grander interest , due care for which every true patriot of the 
 House of Vipont imbibe with his mother 's milk . 

 " your appearance in town , my dear Darrell , be most opportune . it will be 
 an object with the whole family to make the most of you at this come 
 ' CRISIS ; ' I say come , for I believe it must come . your name be still 
 freshly remember ; your position great for have be out of all 
 the scrape of the party the last sixteen or seventeen year : your house 
 should be the nucleus of new combination . do not   forget to send Mills 
 to I ; I will engage your chef and your house - steward to - morrow . I know 
 just the man to suit you . your intention to marry too , just at this 
 moment , be most seasonable ; it will increase the family interest . I may 
 give out that you intend to marry ? " 

 " oh , certainly cry it at Charing Cross . " 

 " a club - room will do as well . I beg ten thousand pardon ; but people 
 will talk about money whenever they talk about marriage . I should not 
 like to exaggerate your fortune : I know it must be very large , and all 
 at your own disposal , eh ? " 

 " every shilling . " 

 " you must have save a great deal since you retire into private life ? " 

 " take that for grant . Dick Fairthorn receive my rent , and look to 
 my various investment ; and I accept he as an indisputable authority 
 when I say that , what with the rental of land I purchase in my poor 
 boy 's lifetime and the interest on my much more lucrative moneyed 
 capital , you may safely whisper to all lady likely to feel interest 
 in that diffusion of knowledge , ' thirty - five thousand a year , and an old 
 fool . ' " 

 " I certainly shall not say an old fool , for I be the same age as 
 yourself ; and if I have thirty - five thousand pound a year , I would marry 
 too . " 

 " you would ! old fool ! " say Darrell , turn away . 




 CHAPTER IV . 

  reveal glimpse of Guy Darrell 's past in his envy prime . dig 
  but deep enough , and under all earth run water , under all life run 
  grief . 

 alone in the street , the vivacity which have characterize Darrell 's 
 countenance as well as his word , while with his old school friend , 
 change as suddenly and as completely into pensive abstract gloom 
 as if he have be act a part , and with the exit the acting cease . 
 disinclined to return yet to the solitude of his home , he walk on at 
 first mechanically , in the restless desire of movement , he care not 
 whither . but as , thus chance - lead , he find himself in the centre of that

 Guy Darrell turn hurriedly from the large house in the great square , 
 and , more and more absorb in revery , he wander out of his direct way 
 homeward , clear and broad though it be , and do not rouse himself till

 tatterdemalion figure , and , see Darrell 's face under the lamp , halt

 dark form fill up the dark aperture . do that ragged wayfarer

 hostile his look , his gesture , the sudden cower down of the strong

 with what intent ? a fierce one , perhaps , - for the man 's face be

 round . there , at the corner near the learn Club - house , halt the 
 tatterdemalion . towards the tatterdemalion the policeman now advance 
 quickly . the tatterdemalion be quick still ; flee like a guilty 
 thought . 

 back , back , back into that maze of passage and court , back to the

 the bravo throw himself at length on one of the table , and , close

 " Souviens - toi de ta Gabrielle , " o Jasper Losely ! but why Arabella

 destitution . but man , however vile , whatever his peril , whatever his 
 destitution , be bear free , and love liberty . liberty to go to Satan 
 in his own way be to Jasper Losely a supreme blessing compare to 
 that benignant compassionate espionage , with its relentless eye and 
 restrain hand . Alas and alas ! deem not this perversity unnatural 
 in that headstrong self - destroyer ! how many be there whom not a 
 grim , hard - feature Arabella Crane , but the long - suffering , divine , 
 omniscient , gentle Providence itself , seek to warn , to aid , to save ; 
 and be shun , and loathe , and flee from , as if it be an evil 
 genius ! how many be there who fear nothing so much as the be make 
 good in spite of themselves ? - how many ? who can count they ? 




 CHAPTER VI . 

  the public man need but one patron ; namely , the LUCKY moment . 

 " at his house in Carlton Gardens , Guy Darrell , Esq . , for the season . " 

 simple insertion in the pompous list of Fashionable Arrivals ! the name 
 of a plain commoner embed in the amber which glitter with so many 
 coronet and star ! yet such be England , with all its veneration for 
 title , that the eye of the public pass indifferently over the rest 
 of that chronicle of illustrious " whereabout , " to rest with interest , 
 curiosity , speculation , on the unemblazoned name which but a day before 
 have seem slip out of date , - obsolete as that of an actor who 
 figure no more in play - bill . unquestionably the sensation excite 
 be due , in much , to the " ambiguous voice " which Colonel Morley have 
 disseminate throughout the genial atmosphere of club - room . " arrive 
 in London for the season ! " - he , the orator , once so famous , long so 
 forget , who have be out of the London world for the space of 
 more than half a generation . " why now ? why for the season ? " Quoth the 
 Colonel , " he be still in the prime of life as a public man , and - a 
 CRISIS be at hand ! " 

 but that which give weight and significance to Alban Morley 's hint 
 be the report in the newspaper of Guy Darrell 's visit to his old 
 constituent , and of the short speech he have address to they , to which 
 he have so slightly refer in his conversation with Alban . true , the 
 speech be short : true , it touch but little on pass topic of 
 political interest ; rather allude , with modesty and terseness , to 
 the contest and victory of a former day . but still , in the few word 
 there be the swell of the old clarion , the wind of the Paladin 's horn 
 which wake fontarabian echo . 

 it be astonish how capricious , how sudden , be the change in value 
 of a public man . all depend upon whether the public want , or believe 
 they want , the man ; and that be a question upon which the public do not 
 know their own mind a week before ; nor do they always keep in the same 
 mind , when make up , for a week together . if they do not want a man ; 
 if he do not hit the taste , nor respond to the exigency of the 
 time , - whatever his eloquence , his ability , his virtue , they push 
 he aside or cry he down . be he want ? do the mirror of the moment 
 reflect his image ? - that mirror be an intense magnifier - his proportion 
 swell ; they become gigantic . at that moment the public want some man ; 
 and the instant the hint be give , " why not Guy Darrell ? " Guy Darrell 
 be seize upon as the man want . it be one of those time in our 
 parliamentary history when the public be out of temper with all 
 party ; when recognize leader have contrive to damage themselves ; 
 when a Cabinet be shake , and the public neither care to destroy nor to 
 keep it , - a time too , when the country seem in some danger , and 
 when , mere man of business hold unequal to the emergency , whatever name 
 suggest association of vigour , eloquence , genius rise to a premium 
 above its market price in time of tranquillity and tape . without effort 
 of his own , by the mere force of the undercurrent , Guy Darrell be 
 throw up from oblivion into note . he could not form a Cabinet , 
 certainly not ; but he might help to bring a Cabinet together , reconcile 
 jarring element , adjust dispute question , take in such government 
 some high place , influence its council , and delight a public weary 
 of the oratory of the day with the eloquence of a former race . for the 
 public be ever a laudator temporis acti , and whatever the author or 
 the orator immediately before it , be those author and orator Homers 
 and Ciceros , would still shake a disparage head , and talk of these 
 degenerate day as Homer himself talk age before Leonidas stand 
 in the pass of Thermopylae , or Miltiades rout asian armament at 
 Marathon . Guy Darrell belong to a former race . the father of those 
 young member rise now into fame have quote to their son his pithy 
 sentence , his vivid image ; and add , as Fox add when quote Burke , 
 " but you should have hear and see the man ! " 

 hear and see the man ! but there he be again ! come up as from a 
 grave , - come up to the public just when such a man be want . want 
 how ? want where ? oh , somehow and somewhere ! there he be ! make the 
 most of he . the house in Carlton Gardens be prepare , the establishment 
 mount . Thither flock all the Viponts , nor they alone ; all the chief 
 of all party , nor they alone ; all the notability of our grand 
 metropolis . Guy Darrell might be startle at his own position ; but he 
 comprehend its nature , and it do not discompose his nerve . he know 
 public life well enough to be aware how much the popular favour be the 
 creature of an accident . by chance he have nick the time ; have he thus 
 come to town the season before , he might have continue obscure , a man 
 like Guy Darrell not be want then . whether with or without 
 design , his bearing confirm and extend the effect produce by his 
 reappearance . gracious , but modestly reserve , he speak little , listen 
 beautifully . many of the question which agitate all around he have 
 grow up into importance since his day of action ; nor in his retirement 
 have he trace their progressive development , with their changeful 
 effect upon man and party . but a man who have once go deeply into 
 practical politic might sleep in the Cave of Trophonius for twenty 
 year , and find , on wake , very little to learn . Darrell regain the 
 level of the day , and seize upon all the strong point on which man 
 be divide , with the rapidity of a prompt and comprehensive intellect , 
 his judgment perhaps the clear from the freshness of long repose and 
 the composure of dispassionate survey . when partisan wrangle as to 
 what should have be do , Darrell be silent ; when they ask what 
 should be do , out come one of his terse sentence , and a knot be 
 cut . meanwhile it be true this man , round whom expectation group and 
 rumour buzz , be in neither House of Parliament ; but that be rather a 
 delay to his energy than a detriment to his consequence . 

 important constituency , anticipate a vacancy , be already on the 
 look - out for he ; a small constituency , in the interim , Carr Vipont 
 undertake to procure he any day . there be always a Vipont ready to 
 accept something , even the Chiltern Hundreds . but Darrell , not without 
 reason , demur at re - enter the House of Commons after an absence of 
 seventeen year . he have leave it with one of those rare reputation 
 which no wise man like rashly to imperil . the Viponts sigh . he would 
 certainly be more useful in the Commons than the Lords , but still in the 
 Lords he would be of great use . they would want a debating lord , perhaps 
 a lord acquaint with law in the come CRISIS , - if he prefer the 
 peerage ? darrell demur still . the man 's modesty be insufferable ; his 
 style of speak might not suit that august assembly : and as to law , 
 he could never now be a law lord ; he should be but a ci - devant advocate , 
 affect the part of a judicial amateur . 

 in short , without decline to re - enter public life , seem , on the 
 contrary , to resume all his interest in it , Darrell contrive with 
 admirable dexterity to elude for the present all overture press upon 
 he , and even to convince his admirer , not only of his wisdom , but 
 of his patriotism in that reticence . for certainly he thus manage to 
 exercise a very considerable influence : his advice be more seek , 
 his suggestion more heed , and his power in reconcile certain rival 
 jealousy be perhaps great than would have be the case if he 
 have actually enter either House of Parliament , and throw himself 
 exclusively into the rank , not only of one party , but of one section of 
 a party . nevertheless , such suspense could not last very long ; he must 
 decide at all event before the next session . once he be see in 
 the arena of his old triumph , on the bench devote to stranger 
 distinguish by the Speaker 's order . there , recognize by the old 
 member , eagerly gaze at by the young , Guy Darrell listen calmly , 
 throughout a long field - night , to voice that must have rouse from 
 forget graf kindling and glorious memory ; voice of those 
 veteran now - by whose side he have once struggle for some cause which 
 he have then , in the necessary exaggeration of all honest enthusiasm , 
 identify with a nation 's life - blood . voice , too of the old 
 antagonist over whose routed argument he have march triumphant amidst 
 applause that the next day ring again through England from side to 
 side . Hark ! the very man with whom , in the old battle - day , he have be 
 the most habitually pit , be speak now ! his tone be embarrassed , 
 his argument confuse . do he know who listen yonder ? old member 
 think so , - smile ; whisper each other , and glance significantly where 
 Darrell sit . 

 sit , as become he , tranquil , respectful , intent , seemingly , perhaps 
 really , unconscious of the sensation he excite . what an eye for an 
 orator ! how like the eye in a portrait ; it seem to fix on each other 
 eye that seek it , - steady , fascinating . Yon distant member , behind the 
 Speaker 's chair , at the far distance , feel the light of that eye travel 
 towards they . how lofty and massive , among all those row of human 
 head , seem that forehead , bend slightly down , with the dark strong 
 line of the weighty eyebrow ! but what be pass within that secret 
 mind ? be there mournfulness in the retrospect ? be there eagerness to 
 renew the strife ? be that interest in the hour 's debate feign or real ? 
 impossible for he who gaze upon that face to say . and that eye would 
 have seem to the gazer to read himself through and through to the 
 heart 's core , long ere the gazer could hazard a single guess as to the 
 thought beneath that marble forehead , - as to the emotion within the 
 heart over which , in old senatorial fashion , the arm be fold with 
 so conventional an ease . 




 CHAPTER VII . 

  Darrell and Lionel . 

 Darrell have receive Lionel with some evident embarrassment , which soon 
 yield to affectionate warmth . he take to the young man whose fortune 
 he have so improve ; he feel that with the improved fortune the young 
 man 's whole being be improve : assure position , early commune with the 
 good social circle , in which the equality of fashion smooth away all 
 disparity in rank , have soften in Lionel much of the wayward and 
 morbid irritability of his boyish pride ; but the high spirit , the 
 generous love of independence , the scorn of mercenary calculation , be 
 strong as ever ; these be in the grain of his nature . in common with 
 all who in youth aspire to be one day note from the " undistinguishable 
 many , " Lionel have form to himself a certain ideal standard , above the 
 ordinary level of what the world be content to call honest , or esteem 
 clever . he admit into his estimate of life the heroic element , not 
 undesirable even in the most practical point of view , for the world be 
 so in the habit of decrying ; of disbelieve in high motif and pure 
 emotion ; of daguerreotype itself with all its ugly wrinkle , 
 strip of the true bloom that brighten , of the true expression that 
 redeem , those defect which it invite the sun to limn , that we shall 
 never judge human nature aright , if we do not set out in life with our 
 gaze on its fair beauty , and our belief in its latent good . in a 
 word we should begin with the Heroic , if we would learn the Human . but 
 though to himself Lionel thus secretly prescribe a certain superiority 
 of type , to be sedulously aim at , even if never actually attain , 
 he be wholly without pedantry and arrogance towards his own 
 contemporary . from this he be save not only by good - nature , animal 
 spirit , frank hardihood , but by the very affluence of idea which 
 animate his tongue , colour his language , and whether to young or 
 old , wise or dull , make his conversation racy and original . he be a 
 delightful companion ; and if he have take much instruction from those 
 old and wise than himself , he so bath that instruction in the fresh 
 fountain of his own lively intelligence , so warm it at his own 
 beat impulsive heart , that he could make an old man 's gleaning from 
 experience seem a young man 's guess into truth . fault he have , of 
 course , - chiefly the fault common at his age ; amongst they , perhaps , 
 the most dangerous be , - firstly , carelessness in money matter ; 
 secondly , a distaste for advice in which prudence be visibly 
 predominant . his taste be not in reality extravagant : but money 
 slip through his hand , leave little to show for it ; and when 
 his quarterly allowance become due , ample though it be , - too ample , 
 perhaps , - debt wholly forget start up to seize hold of it . and 
 debt as yet be manageable be not regard with sufficient horror . 
 pay or put aside , as the case might be , they be merely look upon as 
 bore . youth be in danger till it learn to look upon they as fury . for 
 advice , he take it with pleasure , when clothe with elegance and art , 
 when it address ambition , when it exalt the loftier virtue . but 
 advice , practical and prosy , go in at one ear and out at the other . in 
 fact , with many talent , he have yet no adequate ballast of common - sense ; 
 and if ever he get enough to steady his bark through life 's try 
 voyage , the necessity of so much dull weight must be forcibly stricken 
 home less to his reason than his imagination or his heart . but if , 
 somehow or other , he get it not , I will not insure his vessel . 

 I know not if Lionel Haughton have genius ; he never assume that he have : 
 but he have something more like genius than that prototype , RESOLVE , of 
 which he boast to the artist . he have YOUTH , - real youth , - youth of 
 mind , youth of heart , youth of soul . Lithe and supple as he move before 
 you , with the eye to which light or dew spring at once from a nature 
 vibrate to every lofty , every tender thought , he seem more than 
 young , - the incarnation of youth . 

 Darrell take to he at once . amidst all the engagement crowd on 
 the important man , he contrive to see Lionel daily . and what may seem 
 strange , Guy Darrell feel more at home with Lionel Haughton than with 
 any of his own contemporary , - than even with Alban Morley . to the 
 last , indeed , he open speech with less reserve of certain portion of 
 the past , or of certain project in the future . but still , even there , 
 he adopt a tone of half - playful , half - mournful satire , which might be 
 in itself disguise . Alban Morley , with all his good quality , be a man 
 of the world ; as a man of the world , Guy Darrell talk to he . but it 
 be only a very small part of Guy Darrell the Man , of which the world 
 could say " mine . " 

 to Lionel he let out , as if involuntarily , the more amiable , tender , 
 poetic attribute of his vary , complex , uncomprehended character ; 
 not professedly confide , but not take pain to conceal . hear what 
 worldling would call " Sentiment " in Lionel , he seem to glide softly 
 down to Lionel 's own year and talk ' sentiment " in return . after all , 
 this skilled lawyer , this noted politician , have a great dash of the boy 
 still in he . Reader , do you ever meet a really clever man who have not ? 




 CHAPTER VIII . 

  Saith a very homely proverb   pardon its vulgarity , " you can not make

 Darrell ? "

 world so well ; never make enemy till she make an enemy of poor dear 
 Montfort , but that be natural . by the by , I must write to Caroline . 
 sweet creature ! but how absurd , shut herself up as if she be 
 fret for montfort ! that be so like her mother , - heartless , but full

 here Carr Vipont and Colonel Morley enter the room . " we have just

 " she will never marry a fool . " 

 Guy Darrell be very pleasant at " the small family dinnerparty . " Carr 
 be always popular in his manner ; the true old House of Commons manner ,

 " yes , he be bent on marry again ! I have it from Alban Morley : immense

 propre be flatter . " magnificent , intelligent audience , " think the

 CHAPTER IX . 

  escape from a London drawing - room , flesh once more tingle and 
  blood flow . - Guy Darrell explain to Lionel Haughton why he hold 
  it a duty to be an old fool . 

 Lionel Haughton glide through the disenchanted room , and breathe a 
 long breath of relief when he find himself in the friendless street . 

 as he walk slow and thoughtful on , he suddenly feel a hand upon his 
 shoulder , turn , and see Darrell . 

 " give I your arm , my dear Lionel ; I be tired out . what a lovely night ! 
 what sweet scorn in the eye of those star that we have neglect for 
 yon flare light . " 

 LIONEL . - " be it scorn ? be it pity ? be it but serene indifference ? " 

 DARRELL . - " as we ourselves interpret : if scorn be present in our own 
 heart , it will be see in the disc of Jupiter . Man , egotist though he 
 be , exact sympathy from all the universe . joyous , he say to the sun , 
 ' life - giver , rejoice with I . ' grieve , he say to the moon , ' pensive 
 one , thou sharest my sorrow . ' hope for fame ; a star be its promise ! 

 " mourn for the dead ; a star be the land of reunion ! say to earth , ' I 
 have do with thee ; ' to Time , ' thou hast nought to bestow ; ' and all 
 space cry aloud , ' the earth be a speck , thine inheritance infinity . 
 Time melt while thou sighest . the discontent of a mortal be the 
 instinct that prove thee immortal . ' thus construe Nature , Nature be 
 our companion , our consoler . benign as the playmate , she lend herself 
 to our shift humour . serious as the teacher , she respond to the 
 steady inquiry of reason . mystic and hallow as the priestess , she 
 keep alive by dim oracle that spiritual yearning within we , in which , 
 from savage to sage , - through all dream , through all creed , - thrill 
 the sense of a link with divinity . never , therefore , while confer 
 with nature , be Man wholly alone , nor be she a single companion with 
 uniform shape . ever new , ever various , she can pass from gay to severe , 
 from fancy to science , - quick as thought pass from the dance of a 
 leaf , from the tint of a rainbow , to the theory of motion , the problem 
 of light . but lose nature , forget or dismiss she , make companion , by 
 hundred , of man who ignore she , and I will not say with the poet , 
 ' this be solitude . ' but in the commune , what stale monotony , what weary 
 sameness ! " 

 thus Darrell continue to weave together sentence with sentence , the 
 intermediate connection of meaning often so subtle that when put down on 
 paper it require effort to discern it . but it be his peculiar gift to 
 make clear when speak what in writing would seem obscure . look , manner , 
 each delicate accent in a voice wonderfully distinct in its unrivalled 
 melody , all so aid the sense of mere word that it be scarcely 
 extravagant to say he might have talk an unknown language , and a 
 listener would have understand . but , understand or not , those sweet 
 intonation it be such delight to hear that any one with nerve alive 
 to music would have murmur , " talk on forever . " and in this gift 
 lie one main secret of the man 's strange influence over all who come 
 familiarly into his intercourse ; so that if Darrell have ever bestow 
 confidential intimacy on any one not by some antagonistic idiosyncrasy 
 steel against its charm , and that intimacy have be withdraw , a void 
 never to be refill must have be leave in the life thus rob . 

 stop at his door , as Lionel , rapt by the music , have forget the 
 pain of the revery so bewitchingly break , Darrell detain the hand 
 hold out to he , and say , " no , not yet ; I have something to say to you : 
 come in ; let I say it now . " 

 Lionel bow his head , and in surprised conjecture follow his kinsman 
 up the lofty stair into the same comfortless stately room that have be 
 already describe . when the servant close the door , Darrell sink into 
 a chair . fix his eye upon Lionel with almost parental kindness , and 
 motion his young cousin to sit by his side , close , he thus begin , 

 " Lionel , before I be your age I be marry ; I be a father . I 
 be lonely and childless now . my life have be mould by a solemn 
 obligation which so few could comprehend that I scarce know a man live 
 beside yourself to whom I would frankly confide it . pride of family be a 
 common infirmity , - often petulant with the poor , often insolent with the 
 rich ; but rarely , perhaps , out of that pride do man construct a positive 
 binding duty , which at all self - sacrifice should influence the practical 
 choice of life . as a child , before my judgment could discern how much of 
 vain superstition may lurk in our reverence for the dead , my whole heart 
 be engage in a passionate dream , which my wake existence become 
 vow to realize . my father ! - my lip quiver , my eye moisten as I 
 recall he , even now , - my father ! - I love he so intensely ! - the love 
 of childhood , how fearfully strong it be ! all in he be so gentle , 
 yet so sensitive , - chivalry without its armour . I be his constant 
 companion : he speak to I unreservedly , as a poet to his muse . I weep at 
 his sorrow ; I chafe at his humiliation . he talk of ancestor as he 
 think of they ; to he they be being like the old Lares , - not dead 
 in graf , but image ever present on household hearth . doubtless he 
 exaggerate their worth , as their old importance . obscure , indeed , in 
 the annal of empire , their deed and their power , their decline and 
 fall . not so think he ; they be to his eye the moon - track in the 
 ocean of history , - light on the wave over which they have gleam , - all 
 the ocean elsewhere dark ! with he think I ; as my father speak , his 
 child believe . but what to the eye of the world be this inheritor of 
 a vaunted name ? - a threadbare , slight , rustic pedant ; no station in 
 the very province in which moulder away the last lowly dwelling - place 
 of his line , - by lineage high above most noble , in position below most 
 yeoman . he have learn ; he have genius : but the study to which they 
 be devote only serve yet more to impoverish his scanty mean , and 
 lead rather to ridicule than to honour . not a day but what I see on his 
 soft feature the smart of a fresh sting , the gnawing of a new care . 
 thus , as a boy , feel in myself a strength inspire by affection , 
 I come to he one day as he sit grieve , and kneel to he , say , 
 ' Father , courage yet a little while ; I shall soon be a man , and I swear 
 to devote myself as man to revive the old fading race so prize by you ; 
 to rebuild the House that , by you so loved , be lofty in my eye than 
 all the heraldry of king . ' and my father 's face brighten , and his 
 voice bless I ; and I rise up - ambitious ! " Darrell pause , heave a 
 short , quick sigh , and then rapidly continue , 

 " I be fortunate at the University . that be a day when chief of party 
 look for recruit amongst young man who have give the proof and win 
 the first - fruit of emulation and assiduity ; for statesmanship then be 
 deem an art which , like that of war , need early discipline . I have 
 scarcely leave college when I be offer a seat in Parliament by the 
 head of the Viponts , an old Lord Montfort . I be dazzle but for one 
 moment ; I decline the next . the fallen House of Darrell need wealth ; 
 and parliamentary success , in its high honour , often require 
 wealth , - never give it . it chance that I have a college acquaintance 
 with a young man name Vipont Crooke . his grandfather , one of the 
 numberless vipont , have be compel to add the name of Crooke to his 
 own , on succeed to the property of some rich uncle , who be one of 
 the numberless crooke . I go with this college acquaintance to visit 
 the old Lord Montfort , at his villa near London , and thence to the 
 country - house of the Vipont Crookes . I stay at the last two or three 
 week . while there , I receive a letter from the eld Fairthorn , my 
 father 's bailiff , entreat I to come immediately to Fawley , hint 
 at some great calamity . on take leave of my friend and his family , 
 something in the manner of his sister startle and pain I , - an 
 evident confusion , a burst of tear , - I know not what . I have never 
 seek to win her affection . I have an ideal of the woman I could 
 love , - it do not resemble she . on reach Fawley , conceive the shock 
 that await I . my father be like one heart - stricken . the principal 
 mortgagee be about to foreclose , - Fawley about to pass forever from the 
 race of the Darrells . I see that the day my father be drive from the 
 old house would be his last on earth . what mean to save he ? - how 
 raise the pitiful sum - but a few thousand - by which to release from the 
 spoiler 's gripe those barren acre which all the land of the Seymour or 
 the Gower could never replace in my poor father 's eye ? my sole income 
 be a college fellowship , adequate to all my want , but useless for sale 
 or loan . I spend the night in vain consultation with Fairthorn . there 
 seem not a hope . next morning come a letter from young Vipont Crooke . 
 it be manly and frank , though somewhat coarse . with the consent of 
 his parent he offer I his sister 's hand , and a dowry of L10,000 . 
 he hint , in excuse for his bluntness , that , perhaps from motif of 
 delicacy , if I feel a preference for his sister , I might not deem myself 
 rich enough to propose , and - but it matter not what else he say . you 
 foresee the rest . my father 's life could be save from despair ; his 
 beloved home be his shelter to the last . that dowry would more than 
 cover the paltry debt upon the land . I give myself not an hour to 
 pause . I hasten back to the house to which fate have lead I . but , " 
 say Darrell , proudly , " do not think I be base enough , even with such 
 excuse , to deceive the young lady . I tell she what be true ; that I 
 could not profess to she the love paint by romance - writer and poet ; 
 but that I love no other , and that if she deign to accept my hand , I 
 should studiously consult her happiness and gratefully confide to her my 
 own . " 

 " I say also , what be true , that if she marry I , ours must be for 
 some year a life of privation and struggle ; that even the interest of 
 her fortune must be devoted to my father while he live , though every 
 shilling of its capital would be settle on herself and her child . 
 how I bless she when she accept I , despite my candour ! - how 
 earnestly I pray that I might love and cherish and requite she ! " 
 Darrell pause , in evident suffering . " and , thank Heaven ! I have nothing 
 on that score wherewith to reproach myself ; and the strength of that 
 memory enable I to bear and forbear more than otherwise would have 
 be possible to my quick spirit and my man 's heart . my dear father ! his 
 death be happy : his home be save ; he never know at what sacrifice to 
 his son ! he be gladden by the first honours my youth achieve . he 
 be resign to my choice of a profession , which , though contrary to his 
 antique prejudice , that allow to the representative of the Darrells 
 no profession but the sword , still promise the wealth which would 
 secure his name from perish . he be credulous of my future , as if 
 I have utter not a vow , but a prediction . he have bless my union , 
 without foresee its sorrow . he have embrace my first - bear , - true , it 
 be a girl , but it be one link onward from ancestor to posterity . and 
 almost his last word be these : ' you will restore the race ; you will 
 revive the name ! and my son 's child will visit the antiquary 's grave , 
 and learn gratitude to he for all that his idle lesson teach to your 
 healthier vigour . ' and I answer , ' Father , your line shall not perish 
 from the land ; and when I be rich and great , and lordship spread 
 far round the lowly hall that your life ennoble , I will say to your 
 grandchild , ' honour ye and your son 's son , while a Darrell yet 
 tread the earth , honour he to whom I owe every thought which nerve I 
 to toil for what you who come after I may enjoy . ' 

 " and so the old man , whose life have be so smileless , die smile . " 

 by this time Lionel have steal Darrell 's hand into his own - his heart 
 swell with childlike tenderness , and the tear roll down his 
 cheek . 

 Darrell gently kiss his young kinsman 's forehead , and , extricate 
 himself from Lionel 's clasp , pace the room , and speak on while pace 
 it . 

 " I make , then , a promise ; it be not keep . no child of mine survive to 
 be teach reverence to my father 's grave . my wed life be not happy : 
 its record need no word . of two child bear to I , both be go . my 
 son go first . I have throw my life 's life into he , - a boy of energy , 
 of noble promise . ' t be for he I begin to build that baffle fabric , 
 ' sepulchri immemor . ' for he I buy , acre on acre , all the land within 
 reach of fawley,-land twelve mile distant . I have mean to fill up the 
 intervene space , to buy out a mushroom earl whose wood and cornfield 
 lie between . I be scheme the purchase , scrawl on the county map , 
 when they bring the news that the boy I have just take back to school 
 be dead , - drown bathing on a calm summer eve . no , Lionel . I must go 
 on . that grief I have wrestle with , - conquer . I be widow then . a 
 daughter still leave , - the first - bear , whom my father have bl on his 
 death - bed . I transfer all my love , all my hope , to she . I have no 
 vain preference for male heir . be a race less pure that run on through 
 the female line ? well , my son 's death be merciful compare to - " again 
 Darrell stop , again hurry on . " enough ! all be forgive in the 
 grave ! I be then still in the noon of man 's life , free to form new 
 tie . another grief that I can not tell you ; it be not all conquer 
 yet . and by that grief the last verdure of existence be so blighted 
 that - that - in short , I have no heart for nuptial altar , for the social 
 world . year go by . each year I say , ' next year the wound will be 
 heal ; I have time yet . ' now age be near , the grave not far ; now , if 
 ever , I must fulfil the promise that cheer my father 's death - bed . 
 nor do that duty comprise all my motif . if I would regain healthful 
 thought , manly action , for my remain year , I must feel that one 
 haunt memory be exorcise and forever lay at rest . it can be so 
 only , - whatever my risk of new care , whatever the folly of the hazard 
 at my age , - be so only by - by - " once more Darrell pause , fix his 
 eye steadily on Lionel , and , open his arm , cry out , " forgive I , 
 my noble Lionel , that I be not content with an heir like you ; and do 
 not you mock at the old man who dream that woman may love he yet , and 
 that his own child may inherit his father 's home . " 

 Lionel spring to the breast that open to he ; and if Darrell have 
 plan how good to remove from the young man 's mind forever the 
 possibility of one selfish pang , no craft could have attain his object 
 like that touching confidence before which the disparity between youth 
 and age literally vanish . and , both make equal , both elevate alike , 
 verily I know not which at the moment feel the elder or the young ! two 
 noble heart , intermingle in one emotion , be set free from all time 
 save the present : par each with each , they meet as brother twin - bear .

 " I quite agree with you , Alban ; Honoria Vipont be a very superior young 
 lady . "

 usual to he .

 Edgeworth 's novel ; and in converse with Miss Honoria Vipont , methink 
 I confer with one of Miss Edgeworth 's heroine - so rational , so prudent , 
 so well - behave - so free from silly romantic notion - so replete with 
 solid information , moral philosophy and natural history - so sure to 
 regulate her watch and her heart to the precise moment , for the one 
 to strike , and the other to throb - and to marry at last a respectable 
 steady husband , whom she will win with dignity , and would lose with 
 decorum ! a very superior girl indeed . " 

   " darrell speak - not the author . Darrell be unjust to the more 
  exquisite female character of a Novelist , admirable for strength of

 the friend be rid in the park ; and as Darrell speak , he bow to

 man of high ton , but who belong to that jeunesse doree with which 
 the surface of life patrician be fret over - young man with few idea , 
 few duty - but with plenty of leisure - plenty of health - plenty of 
 money in their pocket - plenty of debt to their tradesman - dare at 
 Melton - scheme at T'attersall 's - pride to maiden aunt - plague to 
 thrifty father - fickle lover , but solid match - in brief , fast

 be middle - aged before they be thirty - tame by wedlock - sober by the

 but meanwhile the elite of this jeunesse doree glitter round Flora

 CHAPTER II . 

  " let observation , with expansive view , 
  survey mankind from China to Peru , " 

  - and OBSERVATION will EVERYWHERE FIND , INDISPENSABLE to the

 Lionel know that Mrs. Haughton would that day need more than usual 
 forewarning of a visit from Mr. Darrell . for the evening of that day 
 Mrs. Haughton propose " to give a party . " when Mrs. Haughton give a 
 party , it be a serious affair . a notable and bustling housewife , she 
 attend herself to each preparatory detail . it be to assist at this

 reluctantly acquiesce in the arrangement by which Alban Morley have

 without gratify his mother 's proud heart by an hour or two spend in

 tempting excursion , with gay comrade . difficult in London life , and at 
 the full of its season , to devote an hour or two to visit , apart from 
 the track chalk out by one 's very mode of existence - difficult to 
 cut off an hour so as not to cut up a day . and Mrs. Haughton be

 CHAPTER III . 

  MRS . HAUGHTON AT HOME to GUY DARRELL . 

 thank to Lionel 's activity , the hall be disencumber - the plant

 CHAPTER V. 

  it be assert by those learned MEN who have devote their life to 
  the study of the manner and HABIT of INSECT SOCIETY , that when a 
  spider have lose its last web , have exhaust all the GLUTINOUS 
  matter wherewith to spin another , it still . protract its INNOCENT 
  EXISTENCE , by obtrude its NIPPERS on some LESS WARLIKE but MORE 
  RESPECTABLE SPIDER , POSSESSED of a convenient home and an AIRY 
  LARDER . OBSERVANT MORALISTS have NOTICED the same peculiarity in 
  the maneater , or pocket - CANNIBAL . 

 Eleven o'clock , A.M. , Samuel Adolphus Poole , Esq . , be in his 
 parlour , - the house one of those new dwelling which yearly spring up 
 north of the Regent 's Park , - dwelling that , attest the eccentricity 
 of the national character , task the fancy of the architect and the 
 gravity of the beholder - each tenement so torture into contrast with 
 the other , that , on one little rood of ground , all age seem blend , 
 and all race encamp . no . 1 be an egyptian tomb ! - pharaoh may repose 
 there ! no . 2 be a swiss chalet - William Tell may be shoot in its 
 garden ! Lo ! the severity of doric column - Sparta be before you ! 
 behold that Gothic porch - you be rapt to the Norman day ! ha ! those 
 Elizabethan mullion - Sidney and Raleigh , rise again ! Ho ! the trellis 
 of China - come forth , Confucius , and Commissioner Yeh ! pass a few 
 pace , we be in the land of the Zegri and Abencerrage : 

  ' Land of the dark - eyed maid and dusky Moor . ' 

 Mr. Poole 's house be call Alhambra Villa ! moorish 
 verandah - plate - glass window , with cuspe head and mahogany sash - a 
 garden behind , a small one in front - stair ascend to the doorway 
 under a saracenic portico , between two pedestalle lion that resemble 
 poodle - the whole new and lustrous - in semblance stone , in substance 
 stucco - crack in the stucco denote ' settlement . " but the house be 
 let for ninety - nine year - relet again on a running lease of seven , 
 fourteen , and twenty - one - the builder be not answerable for duration , 
 nor the original lessee for repair . take it altogether , than 
 Alhambra Villa masonry could devise no well type of modern taste and 
 metropolitan speculation . 

 Mr. Poole , since we see he between four and five year ago , have enter 
 the matrimonial state . he have marry a lady of some money , and become a 
 reform man . he have eschew the turf , relinquish belcher neckcloth 
 and Newmarket coat - drop his old - bachelor acquaintance . when a man 
 marrie and reform , especially when marriage and reform be 
 accompany with increase income , and settle respectably in Alhambra 
 Villa - relation , before estranged , tender kindly overture : the world , 
 before austere , become indulgent . it be so with Poole - no long 
 dolly . grant that in early life he have fall into bad way , and , 
 among equivocal associate , have be lead on by that taste for sporting 
 which be a manly though a perilous characteristic of the true - bear 
 Englishman ; he who love horse be liable to come in contact with 
 blackleg ; the racer be a noble animal ; but it be his misfortune that 
 the well his breed the bad his company : - grant that , in the 
 stable , Adolphus Samuel Poole have pick up some wild oats - he have sow 
 they now . bygone be bygone . he have make a very prudent marriage . 
 Mrs. Poole be a sensible woman - have render he domestic , and would 
 keep he straight ! his uncle Samuel , a most worthy man , have find he 
 that sensible woman , and , have find she , have pay his nephew 's debt , 
 and add a round sum to the lady 's fortune , have see that the whole 
 be so tightly settle on wife and child that Poole have the tender 
 satisfaction of know that , happen what might to himself , those dear 
 one be safe ; nay , that if , in the revers of fortune , he should be 
 compel by persecute creditor to fly his native shore , law could 
 not impair the competence it have settle upon Mrs. Poole , nor destroy 
 her blessed privilege to share that competence with a beloved spouse . 
 Insolvency itself , thus protect by a marriage settlement , realise the 
 sublime security of VIRTUE immortalise by the roman muse : 

  - " Repulse nescia sordidae , 
  Intaminatis fulget honoribus ; 
  Nec sumit ant ponit secure 
  Arbitrio popularis aurae . " 

 Mr. Poole be an active man in the parish vestry - he be a sound 
 politician - he subscribe to public charity - he attend public 
 dinner he have vote in half a dozen public institution - he talk 
 of the public interest , and call himself a public man . he choose his 
 associate amongst gentleman in business - speculative , it be true , 
 but steady . a joint - stock company be set up ; he obtain an official 
 station at its board , couple with a salary - not large , indeed , but 
 still a salary . 

 " the money , " say Adolphus Samuel Poole , " be not my object ; but I like 
 to have something to do . " I can not say how he do something , but no 
 doubt somebody be do . 

 Mr. Poole be in his parlour , read letter and sort paper , before

 Jasper Losely be station at the little gate . Jasper be no long in

 five minute . " 

 the rude visitor follow Poole into the back parlour , and close the

 lean his arm over a chair , his hat still on his head , losely fix

 throw I over , you will find yourself in Queer Street . have you call on

 " do ! nothing I should like well . I be tired out . I want to tell my own

 losely throw himself at length on the sofa -   new morocco with spring
