 inexpressibly mournful : " let the young man live , and the old name die 
 with Guy Darrell . ay , ay ! see how the world side with Youth ! what 
 matter all else so that Youth have its toy ! " again his eye hurry on 
 impatiently till he come to the passage devote to Lady Montfort ; then 
 George see that the paper tremble violently in his hand and that his 
 very lip grow white . " ' serious apprehension , ' " he mutter . " I owe 
 ' consideration to such a friend . ' this man be without a heart ! " 

 he clench the paper in his hand without read far . " leave I 
 this letter , George ; I will give an answer to that and to you before 
 night . " he catch up his hat as he speak , pass into the lifeless 
 picture - gallery , and so out into the open air . George , dubious and 
 anxious , gain the solitude of his own room , and lock the door . 




 CHAPTER III . 

  AT last the great question by torture be fairly apply to GUY 
  DARRELL . 

 what will he do with it ? what will Guy Darrell do with the thought 
 that weigh on his brain , rankle in his heart , perplex his dubious 
 conscience ? what will he do with the Law which have govern his past 
 life ? what will he do with that shadow of a name which , alike in 
 swarm crowd or in lonely burial - place , have spell his eye and 
 lure his step as a beckoning ghost ? what will he do with the PRIDE from 
 which the mask have be so rudely tear ? what will he do with idol so 
 long revere ? be they idol , or be they but symbol and image of holy 
 truth ? what will he do with the torturing problem , on the solution of 
 which depend the honour due to consecrated ash , and the right due to 
 beat heart ? there , restless he go , the arrow of that question in 
 his side - now through the broad waste land - now through the dim wood , 
 pause oft with short quick sigh , with hand sweep across his brow as 
 if to clear away a cloud ; - now snatch from our sight by the evergreen 
 round the tomb in that still churchyard - now emerge slow , with 
 melancholy eye fix on the old roof - tree ! what will he do with it ? the 
 Question of Questions , in which all futurity be open , have he on its 
 rack . what will he do with it ? let we see . 




 CHAPTER IV . 

  Immunis aram si tetigit manu , 
  Non sumptuosa blandior hostia , 
  Mollivit aversa Penates , 
  Farre pio et saliente mica . - HORAT . 

 it be the grey of the evening . Fairthorn be saunter somewhat sullenly 
 along the bank of the lake . he have miss , the last three day , his 
 walk with Sophy - miss the pleasing excitement of talk at she , and 
 of the family in whose obsolete glory he consider her very interest 
 an obtrusive impertinence . he have miss , too , his more habitual and 
 less irritate conversation with Darrell . in short , altogether he be 
 put out , and he vent his spleen on the swan , who follow he along the 
 wave as he walk along the margin , intimate either their affection for 
 himself , or their anticipation of the bread - crumb associate with his 
 image - by the amiable note , half snort and half grunt , to which change 
 of time or climate have reduce the vocal accomplishment of those 
 classical bird , so pathetically melodious in the age of Moschus and on 
 the bank of Cayster . 

 " not a crumb , you unprincipled beggar , " growl the musician . " you 
 imagine that mankind be to have no other thought but that of supply 
 you with luxury ! and if you be ask , in a competitive examination , 
 to define ME , your benefactor , you would say : ' a thing very low in the 
 scale of creation , without wing or even feather , but which providence 
 endow with a peculiar instinct for afford nutritious and palatable 
 addition to the ordinary aliment of Swans ! ' ay , you may grunt ; I wish I 
 have you - in a pie ! " 

 slowly , out through the gap between yon grey crag and the thorn - tree ,

 his pace , expect to meet the doe 's wonte companion . he be not

 " Dear Fellow Hermit , " say Darrell , almost gaily , yet with more than 
 usual affection in his greeting and voice , " you find I just when I want

 green . I have news for you , Fairthorn . you , who know more of my secret 
 than any other man , shall be the first to learn a decision that must 
 bind you and I more together - but not in these scene , Dick . 

  ' Ibimus - ibimus ! 
  - - - - - - - - - - Supremum 
  Carpere iter , comite , parati ! ' " 

 " what do you mean , sir ? " ask Fairthorn . " my mind always misgive I 
 when I hear you quote Horace . some reflection about the certainty of 
 death , or other disagreeable subject , be sure to follow ! " 

 " death ! no , Dick - not now . marriage - bell and joy , Dick ! we shall have a 
 wedding ! " 

 " what ! you will marry at last ! and it must be that beautiful Caroline 
 Lyndsay ! it must - it must ! you can never love another ! you know it , my 
 dear , dear master . I shall see you , then , happy before I die . " 

 " tut , foolish old friend ! " say Darrell , lean his aria tenderly on 
 Fairthorn 's shoulder , and walk on slowly towards the house . " how 
 often must I tell you that no marriage - bell can ring for I ! " 

 " but you have tell I , too , that you go to Twickenham to steal a sight 
 of she again ; and that it be the sight of she that make you resolve to 
 we d no one else . and when I have rail against she for fickleness , have 
 you not nearly frighten I out of my wit , as if no one might rail 
 against she but yourself ? and now she be free - and do you not grant 
 that she would not refuse your hand , and would be true and faithful 
 henceforth ? and yet you insist on be - granite . " 

 " no , Dick , not granite ; I wish I be . " 

 " Granite and pride , " persist Dick , courageously . " if one chip a bit 
 off the granite , one only break one 's spade against the pride . " 

 " Pride - you too ! " mutter Darrell , mournfully ; then aloud : " no , it be 
 not pride now , whatever it might have be even yesterday . but I 
 would rather be rack by all the torture that pious inquisitor ever 
 invent out of compassion for obstinate heretic , than condemn the 
 woman I have so fatally love to a penance the misery of which she 
 can not foresee . she would accept I ? - certainly ! why ! because she think 
 she owe I reparation - because she pity I . and my heart tell I 
 that I might become cruel , and mean , and vindictive , if I be to live 
 day by day with one who create in I , while my life be at noon , a love 
 never know in its morn , and to feel that that love 's sole return be 
 the pity vouchsafe to the nightfall of my age . no ; if she pity , 
 but do not love I , when , eighteen year ago , we part under yonder 
 beech - tree , I should be a dotard to dream that woman 's pity mellow into 
 love as our lock become grey , and Youth turn our vow into ridicule . 
 it be not pride that speak here ; it be rather humility , Dick . but we 
 must not now talk of old age and bygone . youth and marriage - bell , 
 Dick ! know that , I have be for hour ponder how to reconcile with 
 my old - fashioned notion dear Lionel 's happiness . we must think of the 
 living as well as the dead , Dick . I have solve the problem . I be happy , 
 and so shall the young folk be . " 

 " you do not   mean to say that you will consent to - " 

 " yes , to Lionel 's marriage with that beautiful girl , whose parentage 
 we never will ask . great man be their own ancestor ; why not sometimes 
 fair woman ? enough - I consent ! I shall of course secure to my kinsman 
 and his bride an ample fortune . Lionel will have time for his honeymoon 
 before he depart for the war . he will fight with good heart now , Dick . 
 young folk of the present day can not bear up against sorrow , as they 
 be train to do in mine . and that amiable lady who have so much pity 
 for I have , of course , still more pity for a charming young couple for 
 whose marriage she scheme , in order to give I a home , Dick . and rather 
 than she should pine and fall ill , and - no matter ; all shall be settle 
 as it should be for the happiness of the living . but something else must 
 be settle ; we must think of the dead as well as the living ; and this 
 name of Darrell shall be bury with I in the grave beside my father 's . 
 Lionel Haughton will keep to his own name . live the Haughtons ! perish , 
 but with no blot on their shield - perish the Darrells ! why , what be 
 that ? Tears , Dick ? Pooh ! - be a man ! and I want all your strength ; for 
 you , too , must have a share in the sacrifice . what follow be not the 
 dictate of pride , if I can read myself aright . no ; it be the final 
 completion and surrender of the object on which so much of my life have 
 be waste - but a surrender that satisfy my crotchet of honour . 
 at all event , if it be pride in disguise , it will demand no victim in 
 other ; you and I may have a sharp pang - we must bear it , Dick . " 

 " what on earth be come now ? " say Dick , dolefully . 

 " the due to the dead , Richara Fairthorn . this nook of fair England , 
 in which I learn from the dead to love honour - this poor domain of 
 Fawley - shall go in bequest to the College at which I be rear . " 

 " Sir ! " 

 " it will serve for a fellowship or two to honest , bravehearte young 
 scholar . it will be thus , while english institution may last , devote 
 to Learning and Honour . it may sustain for mankind some ambition more 
 generous than mine , it appear , ever be - settle thus , not in mine , but 
 my dear father 's name , like the Darrell Museum . these be my due to the 
 dead , Dick ! and the old house thus become useless . the new house be

 married ; - not a stone stand on stone ! the ploughshare shall pass over

 you will then hasten to join I at Sorrento , that corner of earth on 
 which Horace wish to breathe his last sigh . 

  ' Ille to mecum locus et beatae 
  Postulant arce - ibi - tu ' " 

 " do not , sir , do not . Horace again ! it be too much . " Fairthorn be 
 choke ; but as if the idea present to he be really too monstrous

 joke . " 

 " seriously and solemnly , Richara Fairthorn , " say Darrell , gently

 a squirearch 's lost house we shall both grow ashamed to indulge - there

 CHAPTER V. 

  MORE bounteous RUN river when the ice that locked their flow melt 
  into their water . and when fine NATURES RELENT , their kindness be 
  swelled by the THAW . 

 Darrell escape into the house ; Fairthorn sink upon the ground , and

 then Darrell rise and say : " I shall go up - stair to our sick friend for

 on enter Waife 's room , Darrell go straight forward towards Sophy ,

 Darrell rejoin the scholar . " George , " say he , " be kind enough to tell 
 Alban that you show I his letter . be kind enough also to write to

 " you do ! oh , Mr. Darrell , how I honour you ! " 

 " Nay , I no more deserve honour for consent than I should have

 be just pay . it will be three day , I suppose , before Lionel , have 
 join his new regiment at       , can be here . and then it will be 
 week yet , I believe , before his regiment sail ; and I be all for short

 CHAPTER VII . 

  " Domus et placen Uxor . " 

  FAIRTHORN find nothing placens in the uxor , to WHOM DOMUS be 
  indebted for its destruction . 

 another day ! Lionel be expect to arrive an hour or two after noon . 
 Darrell be in his room - his will once more before he . he have draw up a

 representative of its sepulchre bearer . preparation for departure 
 from the doom dwelling - house have begin . there be large box on the 
 floor ; and favourite volume - chiefly in science or classic - lie pile

 what be really at the bottom of Guy Darrell 's heart ? do he feel 
 reconcile to his decision ? be the virtue of his new self - sacrifice 
 in itself a console reward ? be that cordial urbanity , that cheerful 
 kindness , by which he have be yet more endear himself to his guest , 
 sincere or assume ? as he throw aside his pen , and lean his cheek

 it before , even when in his gloomiest mood ; but it be a melancholy much

 dearest . 

 but in resignation , when complete , there be always a strange relief . 
 despite that melancholy , Darrell be less unhappy than he have be for

 his breast . after all , he have secure , to the good of his judgment , the

 it , he have yet , to use his own word , pay his " due to the dead . " no 
 descendant from a Jasper Losely and a Gabrielle Desmarets will sit as

 infamy and fraud . nor be this consolation that of a culpable pride ; it 
 be buy by the abdication of a pride that have oppose its prejudice 
 to live worth - to live happiness . Sophy would not be punish for

 might replace . what matter to they a mouldering , old , desolate 
 manor - house - a few hundred of pitiful acre ? their child would not

 darksome tree - nor less lively of wit if their school theme be 
 sign in the name , not of Darrell , but Haughton . 

 a slight nervous knock at the door . Darrell have summon Fairthorn ;

 of the new pile may be dispose of , sell , cart away - anyhow , anywhere . 
 those of the old house be sacred - not a brick to be carry from the 
 precinct around it . no ; from foundation to roof , all to be piously

 invention fail . that question be reserve for further consideration . 

 " and why , " say Fairthorn , bluntly and coarsely , urge at least 
 reprieve ; " why , if it must be , not wait till you be no more ? why must

 " because , " answer Darrell , ' such an order , leave by will , would seem 
 a reproach to my heir ; it would wound Lionel to the quick . do in my 
 lifetime , and just after I have give my blessing on his marriage , I can 
 suggest a thousand reason for an old man 's whim ; and my manner alone

 bride . " 

 " I wish she be hang , with all my heart , " mutter Fairthorn , " come 
 here to do such astonishing mischief ! but , sir , I can not   obey you ; 
 ' tis no use talk . you must get some one else . Parson Morley will do 
 it - with pleasure too , no doubt ; or that hobble old man whom I suspect 
 to be a conjurer . who know but what he may get knock on the head as 
 he be look on with his wicked one eye ; and then there will be an end 
 of he , too , which would be a great satisfaction ! " 

 " Pshaw , my dear Dick ; there be no one else I can ask but you . the Parson 
 would argue ; I have   have enough of his arguing ; and the old man be the 
 last whom my own arguing could deceive . Fiat justitia . " 

 " do not , sir , do not ; you be break my heart - ' tis a shame , sir , " 
 sob the poor faithful rebel . 

 " well , Dick , then I must see it do myself ; and you shall go on first

 and - yes - it be cowardly in I to shrink . mine be the task . shame on I

  ' Neque tibia 
  Euterpe cohibet , nec Polyhymnia 
  Lesboum refugit tendere barbiton ! ' " 

 at that last remorseless shaft from the Horatian quiver , " venenatis

 CHAPTER VIII . 

  the FLUTE - PLAYER show how LITTLE music HATH power to SOOTHE the 
  SAVAGE BREAST - of a musician . 

 Fairthorn find himself on the very spot in which , more than five year

 despite the grotesque manner and astounding rudeness of the 
 flute - player , his distress of mind be so evident - there be something

 " to be sure I do . " 

 " you would not wish he to die break - hearted in a foreign land - that 
 old house level to the ground and bury in the lake ? eh , Miss - eh ? " 

 " how can you ask I such question ? " say Sophy , faintly . " do speak 
 plainly , and at once . " 

 " well , I will , Miss. I believe you be a good young lady , after all - and 
 do not   wish really to bring disgrace upon all who want to keep you in the 
 dark , and - " 

 " Disgrace ! " interrupt Sopby ; and her pure spirit rise , and the soft 
 blue eye flash a ray like a shooting - star . 

 " no , I be sure you would not like it ; and some time or other you could 
 not help know , and you would be very sorry for it . and that boy 
 Lionel , who be as proud as Guy Darrell himself when I see he last 
  prouder indeed   - that he should be so ungrateful to his benefactor ! 
 and , indeed , the day may come when he may turn round on you , or on the 
 lame old gentleman , and say he have be disgrace . should not wonder at 
 all ! young folk when they be sweet - hearte only talk about rose and 
 angel , and such - like ; but when husband and wife fall out , as they 
 always do soon or later , they do not   mince their word then , and they 
 just take the sharp thing that they can find at their tongue 's end . 
 so you may depend on it , my dear Miss , that some day or other that young 
 Haughton will say , ' that you lose he the old Manor - house and the old 
 Darrell name , ' and have be his disgrace ; that be the very word , Miss ; I 
 have hear husband and wife say it to each other over and over again . " 

 SOPHY . - " oh , Mr. Fairthorn ! Mr. Fairthorn ! these horrid word can not be 
 mean for I . I will go to Mr. Darrell - I will ask he how I can be a 
 dis - " her lip could not force out the word . 

 FAIRTHORN . - " ay ; go to Mr. Darrell , if you please . he will deny it all ; 
 he will never speak to I again . I do not   care - I be reckless . but it be 
 not the less true that you make he an exile because you may make I a 
 beggar . " 

 SOPHY   wring her hand . - " have you no mercy , Mr. Fairthorn ? will you 
 not explain ? " 

 FAIRTHORN . - " yes , if you will promise to keep it secret at least for the 
 next six month - anything for breathing - time . " 

 SOPHY   impatiently . - " I promise , I promise ; speak , speak . " 

 and then Fairthorn do speak ! he do speak of Jasper Losely - his 
 character - his debasement - even of his midnight visit to her host 's 
 chamber . he do speak of the child fraudulently seek to be thrust 
 on Darrell - of Darrell 's just indignation and loathing . the man be 
 merciless ; though he have not an idea of the anguish he be inflict , 
 he be vent his own anguish . all the mystery of her past life become 
 clear at once to the unhappy girl - all that have be keep from she 
 by protect love . all her vague conjecture now become a dreadful 
 certainty ; - explain now why Lionel have flee she - why he have write 
 that letter , over the content of which she have ponder , with she 
 finger on her lip , as if to hush her own sigh - all , all ! she marry

 such generous , magnanimous affection ! she drive his benefactor , her 
 grandsire 's vindicator , from his own hearth ! she - she - that Sophy who , 
 as a mere infant , have recoil from the thought of playful subterfuge

 do ; indeed , the tormentor , leave to himself , would not have cease till 
 nightfall . 

 " fear not , Mr. Fairthorn , " she say , resolutely ; " Mr. Darrell will be 
 no exile ! his house will not be destroy . Lionel Haughton shall not we d 
 the child of disgrace ! fear not , sir ; all be safe ! " 

 she shed not a tear ; nor be there writ on her countenance that CHANGE , 
 speak of blighted hope , which have pass over it at her young lover 's 
 melancholy farewell . no , now she be support - now there be a virtue 
 by the side of a sorrow - now love be to shelter and save the beloved 
 from disgrace - from disgrace ! at that thought , disgrace fall harmless 
 from herself , as the rain from the plume of a bird . she pass on , her 
 cheek glowing , her form erect . 

 by the porch - door she meet Waife and the Morleys . with a kind of wild 
 impetuosity she seize the old man 's arm , and draw it fondly , clingingly

 in all to each other . George Morley eye her countenance in thoughtful

 towards the garden side of the house . Wheels - the tramp of hoof , 
 full gallop ; and George Morley , look up , exclaim : " ha ! here come 
 Lionel ! and see , Darrell be hasten out to welcome he ! " 




 CHAPTER IX . 

  the letter on which RICHARD FAIRTHORN relied for the defeat of the 
  CONSPIRACY against FAWLEY MANOR - HOUSE . bad aspect for house . the 
  HOUSE of vipont be THREATENED . a physician attempts to MEDICINE to 
  a MIND diseased . a strange COMMUNICATION , which hurry the reader 
  ONTO the next chapter . 

 it have be say that Fairthorn have commit to a certain letter 
 his last desperate hope that something might yet save Fawley from 
 demolition , and himself and his master from an exile 's home in that 
 smile nook of earth to which Horace invite Septimius , as unite 
 the advantage of a mild climate , excellent mutton , capital wine ; and 
 afford to Septimius the prospective privilege of sprinkle a tear 
 over the cinder of his poetical friend while the cinder be yet warm ;

 satisfied with the Fawley southdown - hold in just horror all

 the letter in question be address to Lady Montfort . unscrupulously 
 violate the sacred confidence of his master , the treacherous wretch , 
 after accuse she , in language little more consistent with the respect 
 due to the fair sex than that which he have address to Sophy , of all 
 the desolation that the perfidious nuptial of Caroline Lyndsay have 
 bring upon Guy Darrell , declare that the least Lady Montfort could do 
 to repair the wrong inflict by Caroline Lyndsay , be - not to pity his 
 master ! - that her pity be kill he . he repeat , with some grotesque 
 comment of his own , but on the whole not inaccurately , what Darrell 
 have say to he on the subject of her pity . he then inform she of 
 Darrell 's consent to Lionel 's marriage with Sophy ; in which criminal 
 espousal it be clear , from Darrell 's word , that Lady Montfort have have 
 some nefarious share . in the most lugubrious colour he bring before 
 she the consequence of that marriage - the extinguished name , the 
 demolish dwelling - place , the renunciation of native soil itself . he 
 call upon she , by all that be sacred , to contrive some mean to undo 
 the terrible mischief she have originally occasion , and have recently 
 help to complete . his epistle end by an attempt to conciliate and 
 coax . he revive the image of that wild Caroline Lyndsay , to whom he 
 have never refuse a favour ; whose early sum he have assist to cast 
 up - to whose young idea he have communicate the elementary principle 
 of the musical gamut - to whom he have play on his flute , winter eve 
 and summer noon , by the hour together ; that Caroline Lyndsay , who when 
 a mere child , have lead Guy Darrell where she will , as by a thread of 
 silk . ah , how Fairthorn have leap for joy when , eighteen year ago , he 
 have think that Caroline Lyndsay be to be the sunshine and delight of 
 the house to which she have live to bring the cloud and the grief ! and 
 by all these memory , Fairthorn conjure she either to break off the 
 marriage she have evidently help to bring about , or , fail that , to 
 convince Guy Darrell that he be not the object of her remorseful and 
 affectionate compassion . 

 Caroline be almost beside herself at the receipt of this letter . the 
 picture of Guy Darrell efface his very life from his native land , 
 and destroy the last memorial of his birthright and his home - the 
 conviction of the influence she still retain over his bleak and 
 solitary existence - the experience she have already acquire that the 
 influence fail where she have so fondly hop it might begin to repair 
 and to bless , all overpower she with emotion of yearn tenderness 
 and unmitigated despair . what could she do ? she could not offer herself , 
 again to be reject . she could not write again , to force her penitence 
 upon the man who , while acknowledge his love to be unconquere , have 
 so resolutely refuse to see , in the woman who have once deceive his 
 trust - the Caroline of old ! alas , if he be but under the delusion that 
 her pity be the substitute and not the companion of love , how could she 
 undeceive he ? how say - how write - " accept I , for I love you . " caroline 
 Montfort have no pride of rank , but she have pride of sex ; that pride have 
 be call forth , encouraged , strengthen , throughout all the year 
 of her wed life . for Guy Darrell 's sake , and to he alone , that pride 
 she have cast away - trample upon ; such humility be due to he . but when 
 the humility have be once in vain , could it be repeat - would it 
 not be debasement ? in the first experiment she have but to bow to his 
 reproach - in a second experiment she might have but to endure his 
 contempt . yet how , with her sweet , earnest , affectionate nature - how she 
 long for one more interview - one more explanation ! if chance could but 
 bring it about ; if she have but a pretext - a fair reason , apart from any 
 interest of her own , to be in his presence once more ! but in a few day 
 he would have leave England forever - his heart yet more hardened in its 
 resolve by the last sacrifice to what it have so sternly recognise to 
 be a due to other . never to see he more - never to know how much 
 in that sacrifice he be suffer now - would perhaps suffer more 
 hereafter , in the reaction that follow all strain upon purpose - and yet 
 not a word of comfort from she - she who feel bear to be his comforter . 

 but this marriage , that cost he so much , must that be ? could she dare , 
 even for his sake , to stand between two such fair young life as those 
 of Lionel and Sophy - confide to they what Fairthorn have declare - appeal 
 to their generosity ? she shrink from inflict such intolerable sorrow . 
 could it be her duty ? in her inability to solve this last problem , 
 she bethink herself of Alban Morley ; here , at least , he might give 
 advice - offer suggestion . she send to his house entreat he to call . 
 her messenger be some hour before he find the Colonel , and then 
 bring back but a few hasty line - " impossible to call that day . the 
 CRISIS have come at last ! the Country , the House of Vipont , the British 
 Empire , be tremble in the balance . the Colonel be engage every 
 moment for the next twelve hour . he have the Earl of Montfort , who be 
 intractable and stupid beyond conception , to see and talk over ; Carr 
 Vipont be hard at work on the material for the new Cabinet - Alban 
 be help Carr Vipont . if the House of Vipont fail England at this 
 moment , it would not be a CRISIS , but a crash ! the Colonel hop to 
 arrange an interview with Lady Montfort for a minute or two the next 
 day . but perhaps she would excuse he from a journey to Twickenham , and 
 drive into town to see he ; if not at home , he would leave word where he 
 be to be find . " 

 by the beard of Jupiter Capitolinus , there be often revolution in the 
 heart of woman , during which she be callous to a Crisis , and have not 
 even a fear for a crash ! 

 the next day come George 's letter to Caroline , with the gentle message 
 from Darrell ; and when Dr. F - - - , whose apprehension for the state 
 of her health Colonel Morley have by no mean exaggerated , call in the 
 afternoon to see the effect of his last prescription , he find she in 
 such utter prostration of nerve and spirit , that he resolve to 
 hazard a dose not much know to great lady - viz . three grain of 
 plain - speak , with a minim of frightening . 

 " my dear lady , " say he , " yours be a case in which physician can be of 
 very little use . there be something on the mind which my prescription 
 fail to reach ; worry of some sort - decidedly worry . and unless you 
 yourself can either cure that , or will make head against it , worry , 
 my dear Lady Montfort , will end not in consumption - you be too finely 
 form to let worry eat hole in the lung - no ; but in a confirm 
 aneurism of the heart , and the first sudden shock might then be 
 immediately fatal . the heart be a noble organ - bear a great deal - but 
 still its endurance have limit . heart - complaint be more common than 
 they be ; - over - education and over - civilisation , I suspect . very young 
 people be not so subject to they ; they have flurry , not worry - a very 
 different thing . a good chronic silent grief of some year stand , 
 that get worried into acute inflammation at the age when feel be no 
 long fancy , throw out a heart - disease which sometimes kill without 
 warning , or sometimes , if the grief be remove , will rather prolong than 
 shorten life , by induce a prudent avoidance of worry in future . there 
 be that worthy old gentleman who be take so ill at Fawley , and about 
 whom you be so anxious : in his case there have certainly be chronic 
 grief ; then come acute worry , and the heart could not get through its 
 duty . fifty year ago doctor would have cry ' apoplexy ! ' - nowadays 
 we know that the heart save the head . well , he be more easy in his 
 mind the last time I see he , and thank to his temperance , and his 
 constitutional dislike to self - indulgence in worry , he may jog on to 
 eighty , in spite of the stethoscope ! excess in the moral emotion give 
 heart - disease ; abuse of the physical power , paralysis ; both more common 
 than they be - the first for your gentle sex , the second for our rough 
 one . both , too , lie in wait for their victim at the entrance in middle 
 life . I have a very fine case of paralysis now ; a man build up by nature 
 to live to a hundred - never see such a splendid formation - such bone and 
 such muscle . I would have give Van Amburgh the two good of his lion , 
 and my man would have do for all three in five minute . all the bad 
 for he , my dear lady - all the bad for he . his strength lead he on 
 to abuse the main fountain of life , and out jump avenge Paralysis 
 and fell he to earth with a blow . ' tis your Hercules that paralysis 
 love ; she despise the weak invalid , who prudently shim all excess . 
 and so , my dear lady , that assassin call Aneurism lie in wait for the 
 heart that abuse their own force of emotion ; spar heart that , less 
 vital , be thrifty in waste and supply . but you be not listen to 
 one ! and yet my patient may not be quite unknown to your ladyship ; for 
 in happen to mention the other day , to the lady who attend to and 
 nurse he , that I could not call this morning , as I have a visit to pay

 write this note , which she beg I to give you . she seem very much 
 attach to my patient - not his wife nor his sister . she interest 
 I ; - capital nurse - cleverish woman too . oh ! here be the note . " 

 Caroline , who have give but little heed to this recital , listlessly

 mean , say : " no , my dear lady , I promise that I would see you read the 
 note ; besides , I be the most curious of man , and die to know a little 
 more who and what be the writer . " 

 Caroline break the seal and read as follow : " if Lady Montfort remember 
 Arabella Fossett , and will call at Clare Cottage , Vale of Health , 
 Hampstead , at her ladyship 's early leisure , and ask for Mrs. Crane , 
 some information , not perhaps important to Lady Montfort , but very 
 important to Mr. Darrell , will be give . " 

 Lady Montfort startle the doctor by the alertness with which she spring 
 to her foot and ring the bell . 

 " what be it ? " ask he . 

 " the carriage immediately , " cry Lady Montfort as the servant enter . 

 " ah ! you be go to see the poor lady , Mrs. Crane , eh ? well , it be a

 well . pray what relation be Mrs. Crane to my patient ? " 

 " I really do not   know ; pray excuse I , my dear Dr. F - - - - . " 

 " certainly ; go while the day be fine . wrap up ; - a close carriage , 
 mind ; - and I will look in to - morrow . " 




 CHAPTER X. 

  wherein be insinuate the HIGHEST compliment to woman ever pay to 
  her sex by the AUTHOR of this work . 

 Lady Montfort have arrive at Clare Cottage . she be show by Bridget 
 Greggs into a small room upon the first floor ; fold - door to some 
 other room closely shut - evidence of sickness in the house ; - phial on 
 the chimneypiece - a tray with a broth - basin on the table - a saucepan on 
 the hob - the sofa one of those that serve as a bed , which sleep little 
 visit , for one who may watch through the night over some helpless 
 sufferer - a woman 's shawl throw carelessly over its hard narrow 
 bolster ; - all , in short , betray that pathetic untidiness and 
 discomfort which say that a despot be in the house to whose will order 
 and form be subordinate ; - the imperious Tyranny of Disease establish 
 itself in a life that , within those four wall , have a value not to be 
 measure by its worth to the world beyond . the more feeble and helpless 
 the sufferer , the more sovereign the despotism - the more submissive the 
 servitude . 

 in a minute or two one of the folding - door silently open and as

 Fossett , of whose handsome , if somewhat too strongly define and sombre

 Caroline 's lovely head in mute respect . 

 MRS . CRANE . - " you too be change since I see you last , - that be more

 we of former day . I can not tell how , but from that moment a something 
 more like womanhood , than I have know for year , enter into my heart .

 " Hush , do you hear a moan ? - no ! he lie yonder , Caroline Lyndsay - mine ,

 he rest in their clasp , helpless as an infant . " Involuntarily Caroline

 " I say to Guy Darrell that I would learn , if possible , whether the poor 
 child whom I ill - use in my most wicked day , and whom you , it seem , 
 have so benignly shelter , be the daughter of Matilda - or , as he 
 believe , of a yet more hateful mother . long ago I have conceive a

 for I have chance to see two letter address to he - one from

 baleful - in which she speak of some guilty plunder with which she be 
 come to London , and invite he again to join his fortune with her

 bent on repair to Sophy my cruelty to her childhood . what if I have

 and even my suspicion as to her birth die away . pass on : - Guy Darrell ! 
 ah , Lady Montfort ! his life have be embitter like mine ; but he be 
 man , and could bear it well . he have know , himself , the misery of 
 break faith , of betray affection , which he could pity so little when 
 its blight fall on I ; but you have excuse for desertion - you yourself 
 be deceive ; and I pardon he , for he pardon Jasper , and we be 
 fellow - sufferer . you weep ! pardon my rudeness . I do not mean to pain

 cross to France . I see the nurse ; I have ascertain the truth ; here

 the child , or rather of extort money for the renunciation of a claim 
 to one whom you have adopt . I tell he how vainly he have hitherto

 a feeble voice , like the treble of an infirm old man , come painfully to 
 Caroline 's ear .

 so - cruel ! "

 " come in - say you forgive he ! oh , you need not fear he ; a babe could 
 not fear he now ! " 

 Caroline follow Arabella into the sick - room . no untidiness there ; all 
 so carefully , thoughtfully arrange . a pleasant room , too - with window 
 look full on the sunny side of the Vale of Health ; the hearth 
 so cheerily clear , sweep so clean - the very ash out of sight ;

 yes , there , too weak indeed for a babe to fear , lie all that be leave 
 of the Strong Man ! no enemy but himself have bring he thus 
 low - spendthrift , and swindler , and robber of his own priceless 
 treasure - Health and Strength - those grand rent - roll of joy which 
 Nature have make his inheritance . as a tree that be crumble to dust 
 under the gnarl of its bark seem , the moment ere it fall , proof 
 against time and the temp , so , within all decay , stand that image 
 of strength - so , air scarcely stirring , it fall . " and the pitcher be 
 break at the fountain ; and the wheel be break at the cistern ; vanity 
 of vanity , saith the Preacher . " 

 Jasper turn his dull eye towards Caroline , as she come softly to 
 his side , and look at she with a piteous gaze . the stroke that have 
 shatter the form have spar the face ; and illness and compulsory 
 abstinence from habitual stimulant have take from the aspect much 
 of the coarseness - whether of shape or colour - that of late year have 
 disfigure its outline - and supply the delicacy which end with youth 
 by the delicacy that come with the approach of death . so that , in no 
 small degree , the beauty which have be to he so fatal a gift , be once 
 more visible - the feature grow again distinct , as wanness succeed 
 to the hue of intemperance , and emaciation to the bloated cheek and 
 swollen muscle . the goddess whose boon adorn the outward shell of the 
 human spirit come back to her favourite 's death - couch as she have come to 
 the cradle - not now as the Venus Erycina , goddess of Smile and Jest , but 
 as the warning Venus Libitina , the goddess of Doom and the Funeral . 

 " I be a very poor creature , " say Jasper , after a pause . " I can not   rise - I 
 can not   move without help . very strange supernatural ! she always say that 
 if I raise my hand against she , it would fall palsy ! " he turn his 
 eye towards Arabella with a glare of angry terror . " she be a witch ! " 
 he say , and bury his face in the pillow . tear roll down the grim 
 woman 's cheek . 

 LADY MONTFORT . - " she be rather your good minister spirit . do not be 
 unkind to she . over she you have more power now than you have when you 
 be well and strong . she live but to serve you ; command she gently . "

 " Mr. Darrell - "

 CHAPTER XI . 

  the CRISIS - PUBLIC and private . 

 Lady Montfort 's carriage stop at Colonel Morley 's door just as Carr 
 Vipont be come out . Carr , catch sight of she , bustle up to the 
 carriage window . 

 " my dear Lady Montfort ! - not see you for an age ! what time we live 
 in ! how suddenly the CRISIS have come upon we ! sad loss in poor dear 
 Montfort ; no wonder you mourn for he ! have his failing , true - who be 
 not mortal ? - but always vote right ; always to be rely on in time 
 of CRISIS ! but this crotchety fellow , who have so unluckily , for all but 
 himself , walk into that property , be the loose fish ! and what be 
 a House divide against itself ? never be the Constitution in such 
 peril ! - I say it deliberately ! - and here be the Head of the vipont 
 humming and haae , and ask whether Guy Darrell will join the 
 Cabinet . and if Guy Darrell will not , we have no more chance of the 
 Montfort interest than if we be Peep - o'-day Boys . but excuse I ; I 
 must be off ; every moment be precious in time of CRISIS . think , if we 
 can not   form a Cabinet by to - morrow night - only think what may happen ; the 
 other fellow will come in , and then - the DELUGE ! " 

 Carr be go to find mop and Dame Partingtons to stave off the deluge . 
 Colonel Morley have obey Lady Montfort 's summon , and have enter the 
 carriage . before she can speak , however , he have rush into the subject 
 of which he himself be full . " only think - I know it would be so when the 
 moment come ; all depend upon Guy Darrell ; Montfort , who seem always 
 in a fright lest a newspaper should fall on his head and crush he , say 
 that if Darrell , whom he choose to favour just because the newspaper 
 do , decline to join , the newspaper will say the CRISIS be a job ! 
 Fancy ! - a job - the CRISIS ! Lord Mowbray de l'Arco and Sir Josiah Snodge , 
 who be both necessary to a united government , but who unluckily det 
 each other , refuse to sit in the same Cabinet , unless Darrell sit 
 between - to save they , I suppose , from the fate of the cat of Kilkenny . 
 Sir John Cautly , our crack county member , declare that if Darrell do 
 not come in , ' tis because the CRISIS be go too far ! Harry Bold , our 
 most popular speaker , say , if Darrell stay out , ' tis a sign that the 
 CRISIS be a retrograde movement ! in short , without Darrell the 
 CRISIS will be a failure , and the House of Vipont smash - Lady 
 Montfort - smash ! I send a telegram   oh , that I should live to see such 
 a word introduce into the english language ! - but , as Carr say , what 
 time these be !   to Fawley this morning , entreat Guy to come up to 
 town at once . he answer by a line from Horace , which mean , ' that he 
 will see I shot first . ' I must go down to he ; only wait to know 
 the result of certain negotiation as to measure . I have but one hope . 
 there be a measure which Darrell always privately advocate - which 
 he thoroughly understand - which , place in his hand , would be 
 triumphantly carry ; one of those measure , Lady Montfort , which , if 
 defective , shipwreck a government ; if frame as Guy Darrell could frame 
 it , immortalise the minister who concoct and carry they . this be all 
 that Darrell need to complete his fame and career . this be at length an 
 occasion to secure a durable name in the history of his country ; let 
 he reject it , and I shall tell he frankly that his life have be but 
 a brilliant failure . since he have not a seat in Parliament , and usage 
 require the actual possession of that qualification for a seat in the 
 Cabinet , we must lose his voice in the Commons . but we can arrange that ; 
 for if Darrell will but join the government , and go - to the Lords , 
 Sir Josiah Snodge , who have a great deal of voice and a great deal of 
 jealousy , will join too - head the Vipont interest in the Commons - and 
 speak to the country - speak every night - and all night , too , if 
 require . yes ; Darrell must take the peerage - devote himself for a year 
 or two to this great measure - to the consolidation of his fame - to the 
 redemption of the House of Vipont - and to the Salvation of the empire ; 
 and then , if he please , ' solve senescentem ' - that is , he may retire from 
 harness , and browse upon laurel for the rest of his day ! " 

 Colonel Morley deliver himself of this long address without 
 interruption from a listener interested in every word that relate to 
 Guy Darrell , and in every hope that could reunite he to the healthful 
 activity of life . 

 it be now Lady Montfort 's turn to speak ; though , after subject so 
 momentous as the Crisis and its speculative consequence , private 
 affair , relate to a poor little girl like Sophy - nay , the mere 
 private affair of Darrell himself , seem a pitiful bathos . Lady 
 Montfort , however , after a few word of womanly comment upon the only 
 part of the Colonel 's discourse which touch her heart , hasten on to 
 describe her interview with Arabella , and the melaneboly condition of 
 Darrell 's once formidable son - in - law . for that last , the Colonel evince 
 no more compassionate feeling than any true Englishman , at the time I be 
 writing , would demonstrate for a murderous Sepoy tie to the mouth of a 
 cannon . 

 " a very good riddance , " say the Colonel , dryly . " great relief to 
 Darrell , and to every one else whom that monster torment and prey 
 on ; and with his life will vanish the only remain obstacle in 
 right poor Willy 's good name . I hope to live to collect , from all 
 part of the country , Willy 's old friend and give they a supper , at

 than not ! but I interrupt you ! go on . " 

 Lady Montfort proceed to state the substance of the paper she have 
 peruse in reference to the mystery which have be the cause of so much 
 disquietude and bitterness . 

 the Colonel stretch out his hand eagerly for the document - thus 
 quote . he hurry his eye rapidly over the content of the first paper 
 he light on , and then say , pull out his watch : " well , I have half an 
 hour yet to spare in discuss these matter with you - may I order your 
 coachman to drive round the Regent 's Park ? - well than keep it thus 
 at my door , with four old maid for opposite neighbour . " the order be 
 give , and the Colonel again return to the paper . suddenly he look

 gaze , which make her drop her own eye ! and she see that he have be

 neglect to abstract from the other document . " oh , not that paper - you 
 be not to read that , " she cry , quickly cover the writing with her 
 hand . 

 " too late , my dear cousin . I have read it . all be now clear . Lionel be 
 right ; and I be right too , in my conviction , though Darrell put so 
 coolly aside my question when I be last at Fawley . I be justify 
 now in all the pain I take to secure Lionel 's marriage - in the cunning 
 cruelty of my letter to George ! Know , Lady Montfort , that if Lionel 
 have sacrifice his happiness to respect for Guy 's ancestor - worship , Guy 
 Darrell would have hold himself bind in honour never to marry again . he 
 tell I so - tell I he should be a cheat if he take any step to rob one 
 from whom he have exact such an offering - of the name , and the heritage , 
 for which the offering have be make . and I then resolve that County 
 Guy should not thus irrevocably shut the door on his own happiness ! Lady 
 Montfort , you know that this man love you - as , verily , I believe , never 
 other man in our cold century love woman ; - through desertion - through 
 change - amidst grief - amidst resentment - despite pride ; - dead to all 
 other love - shrink from all other tie - on , constant on - carrying in 
 the depth of his soul to the verge of age , secret and lock up , the 
 hopeless passion of his manhood . do you not see that it be through you , 
 and you alone , that Guy Darrell have for seventeen year be lose to the 
 country he be intend to serve and to adorn ? do you not feel that 
 if he now reject this last opportunity to redeem year so waste , and 
 achieve a fame that may indeed link his Ancestral Name to the honour of 
 Posterity , you , and you alone , be the cause ? " 

 " Alas - alas - but what can I do ? " 

 " do ! - ay , true . the poor fellow be old now ; you can not care for 
 he ! - you still young , and so unluckily beautiful ! - you , for whom young 
 prince might vie . true ; you can have no feeling for Guy Darrell , except 
 pity ! " 

 " Pity ! I hate the word ! " cry Lady Montfort , with as much petulance as 
 if she have still be the wayward lively Caroline of old . 

 again the Man of the World direct toward her face his shrewd eye , and 
 drop out , " see he ! " 

 " but I have see he . you remember I go to plead for Lionel and 
 Sophy - in vain ! " 

 " not in vain . George write I word that he have inform you of 
 Darrell 's consent to their marriage . and I be much mistaken if his 
 great consolation in the pang that consent must have cost he be not 
 the thought that it relieve you from the sorrow and remorse his refusal 
 have occasion to you . ah ! there be but one person who can restore 
 Darrell to the world - and that be yourself ! " 

 Lady Montfort shake her head drearily . 

 " if I have but an excuse - with dignity - with self - respect - to - to - ! ' 

 " an excuse ! you have an absolute necessity to communicate with Darrell . 
 you have to give he these document - to explain how you come by they . 
 Sophy be with he ; you be bind to see she on a subject of such vital

 nearly ! and why , forsooth ? because , age ago , your hand be , it seem , 
 engage to he , and you be deceive by false appearance , like a silly 
 young girl as you be . " 

 again Lady Montfort shake her head drearily - drearily . " well , " say the 
 Colonel , change his tone , " I will grant that those former tie can not 
 be renew now . the man now be as old as the hill , and you have no right

 jilt for a handsome young Marquess . " 

 " Cease , sir , cease , " cry Caroline , angrily . the Colonel coolly 
 persist . 

 " I see now that such nuptial be out of the question . but have the world 
 come to such a pass that one can never at any age have a friend in a 
 lady unless she marry he ? Scruple to accompany I - I your cousin - I

 you have virtually adopt ! - scruple to trust yourself for half an hour 
 to that tumbledown old Fawley ! be you afraid that the gossip will 
 say you , the Marchioness of Montfort , be run after a gloomy old 
 widower , and scheme to be mistress of a mansion more like a ghosttrap

 Darrell will be fool and fop enough to think you be come to force on

 even a suspicious old roue . but Guy Darrell - a man of his station , his 
 character , his year ! and you , cousin Caroline , what be you ? surely , 
 lift above all such pitiful crotchet by a rank amongst the lofty 
 gentlewoman of England ; ample fortune , a beauty that in itself be 
 rank and wealth ; and , above all , a character that have pass with such

 every ear invite a scandal . but as you will . all I say be , that 
 Darrell 's future may be in your hand ; that after to - morrow , the

 lose ; and that I do believe , if you say to - morrow to Guy Daxrell , ' you 
 refuse to hear I when I plead for what you think a disgrace

 affection as if of duty - now hear I when I plead by the side of 
 your old friend on behalf of your honour , and in the name of your 
 forefather , ' - if you say that , he be win to his country . you will have 
 repair a wrong ; and , pray , will you have compromise your dignity ? " 

 Caroline have recoil into the corner of the carriage , her mantle close 
 down round her breast , her veil lower ; but no shelter garb or veil 
 could conceal her agitation . 

 the Colonel pull the check - string . " nothing so natural ; you be the 
 widow of the Head of the House of Vipont . you be , or ought to be , 
 deeply interested in its fate . an awful CRISIS , long expect , have 
 occur . the House tremble . a connection of that House can render it

 childhood be rear ; and you go with I - I , who be know to be move

 these document . I will see to the rest of this long painful subject . I 
 will send a special report to you this evening , and you will reply by a 
 single line to the prayer I have venture to address to you . " 




 CHAPTER XII . and last . 

  in which the AUTHOR ENDEAVOURS , to the best of his ABILITY , to give 
  A final reply to the question , " what will he do with it ? " 

 SCENE - the bank of the lake at Fawley . George be lend his arm to 
 Waife ; Mrs. Morley , seat on her camp - stool at the opposite side of the 
 water , be put the last touch to her sketch of the Manor - house ; Sir 
 Isaac , recline , be gravely contemplate the swan ; the doe , bend 
 over he , occasionally nibble his ear ; Fairthorn have uncomfortably 
 edge himself into an angle of the building , between two buttress , and 
 be watch , with malignant eye , two young form , at a distance , as they 
 move slowly yonder , side by side , yet apart , now lose , now emerge , 
 through the gap between melancholy leafless tree . darrell , have 
 just quit Waife and George , to whose slow pace he can ill time his 
 impatient step , wonder why Lionel , whom , on arrive , he have , with 
 brief cordial word , refer to Sophy for his fate , have take more than 
 an hour to ask a simple question , to which the reply may be pretty well 
 know beforehand . he advance towards those melancholy tree . suddenly 
 one young form leave the other - come with rapid stride through the 
 wither fern . Pale as death , Lionel seize Guy Darrell 's hand with 
 convulsive grasp , and say : " I must leave you , sir ; God bless you ! all 
 be over . I be the blind fool - she refuse I . " 

 " refuse you ! - impossible ! for what reason ? " 

 " she can not love I well enough to marry , " answer Lionel with a 
 quivering lip , and an attempt at that irony in which all extreme 
 anguish , at least in our haughty sex , delight to seek refuge or 
 disguise . " like I as a friend , a brother , and so forth , but - nothing 
 more . all a mistake , sir - all , except your marvellous kindness to I - to 
 she - for which Heaven ever bless you . " 

 " yes , all a mistake of your own , foolish boy , " say Darrell , tenderly ; 
 and , turn sharp , he see Sophy hasten by , quickly and firmly , with 
 her eye look straightward - on into space . he throw himself in her 
 path . 

 " tell this dull kinsman of mine that ' faint heart never win fair lady . ' 
 you do not mean seriously , deliberately to reject a heart that will 
 never be faint with a mean fear than that of lose you ? " 

 Poor Sophy ! she keep her blue eye still on the cold grey space , and 
 answer by some scarce audible word - word which in every age girl 
 intend to say no seem to learn as bird learn their song ; no one 
 know who teach they , but they be ever to the same tune . " sensible of 
 the honour " - " grateful " - " some one more worthy , "   c. ,   c. 

 Darrell check this embarrassed jargon . " my question , young lady , be 
 solemn ; it involve the destiny of two life . do you mean to say that 
 you do not love Lionel Haughton well enough to give he your hand , and 
 return the true faith which be pledge with his own ? " 

 " yes , " say Lionel , who have gain the side of his kinsman , " yes , that 
 be it . o Sophy - Ay or no ? " 

 " no ! " fall from her pale , firm lip - and in a moment more she be 
 at Waife 's side , and have draw he away from George . " Grandfather , 
 grandfather ! - home , home ; let we go home at once , or I shall die ! " 

 Darrell have keep his keen sight upon her movement - upon her 
 countenance . he see her gesture - her look - as she now cling to her 
 grandfather . the blue eye be not now coldly fix on level air , but 
 raise upward as for strength from above . the young face be sublime with 
 its woe , and with its resolve . 

 " noble child , " mutter Darrell , " I think I see into her heart . if so , 
 poor Lionel , indeed ! my pride have yield , hers never will ! " 

 Lionel , meanwhile , keep beat his foot on the ground , and check 
 indignantly the tear that seek to gather to his eye . Darrell throw 
 his arm round the young man 's shoulder , and lead he gently , slowly away , 
 by the barbed thorn - tree - on by the moss - grow crag . 

 Waife , meanwhile , be bend his ear to Sophy 's lip . the detestable 
 Fairthorn emerge from between the buttress , and shamble up to 
 George , thirst to hear his hope confirm , and turn his face back 
 to smile congratulation , on the gloomy old house that he think he have 
 save from the lake . 

 Sophy have at last convince Waife that his sens do not deceive he , 
 nor hers wander . she have say , " o grandfather , let we ever henceforth 
 be all in all to each other . you be not ashamed of I - I be so proud 
 of you . but there be other akin to I , grandfather , whom we will not 
 mention ; and you would be ashamed of I if I bring disgrace on one who 
 would confide to I his name , his honour ; and should I be as proud of 
 you , if you ask I to do it ? " 

 at these word , Waife understand all , and he have not an argument in 
 reply ; and he suffer Sophy to lead he towards the house . yes , they 
 will go hence - yes , there shall be no scheme of marriage ! they have 
 nearly reach the door , when the door itself open violently , and a 
 man rush forth catch Sophy in his arm , and kiss her forehead , 
 her cheek , with a heartiness that it be well Lionel do not witness ! 
 Speechless and breathless with resentment , Sophy struggle , and in vain , 
 when Waife , seize the man by the collar , swing he away with a " how 
 dare you , sir , " that be echo back from the hillock - summon 
 Sir Isaac at full gallop from the lake - scar Fairthorn back to his 
 buttress - rouse Mrs. Morley from her sketch , and , smite the ear of 
 Lionel and Darrell , hurry they , mechanically as it be , to the very 
 spot from which that thunder - roll have peal . 

 " how dare I ? " say the man , resettle the flow of his disorder 
 coat - " how dare I kiss my own niece ? - my own sister 's orphan child ? 
 Venerable Bandit , I have a much well right than you have . oh , my dear 
 injure Sophy , to think that I be ashamed of your poor cotton print - to 
 think that to your pretty face I have be owe fame and fortune - and 
 you , you wander over the world - child of the sister of whose beauty 
 I be so proud - of she for whom , alas , in vain ! I paint Watteaus and 
 Greuzes upon screen and fan ! " again he clasp she to his breast ; and 
 Waife this time stand mute , and Sophy passive - for the man 's tear be 
 rain upon her face , and wash away every blush of shame as to the 
 kiss they hallow . 

 " but where be my old friend William Losely ? - where be Willy ? " say 
 another voice , as a tall , thin personage step out from the hall , and 
 look poor Waife unconsciously in the face . 

 " Alban Morley ! " falter Waife , " you be but little change ! " 

 the Colonel look again , and in the elderly , lame , one - eye , 
 sober - looking man , recognise the wild jovial Willy , who have tame the 
 most unruly filly , take the most frantic leap , carol forth the 
 blithe song - madcap , good - fellow , frolicsome , childlike darling of gay 
 and grave , young and old ! 

  " ' Eheu , fugace , Postume , Postume , 
  Labuntur anni , ' " 

 say the Colonel , insensibly imbibe one of those horatian particle 
 that be ever float in that classic atmosphere - to Darrell 
 medicinal , to fairthorn morbific . " year slide away , Willy , mutely as

 we be not too old yet , Willy , for many a meet at the fireside ! nothing

 hobby contrive to be in at the death . 

 " but you be wait to learn by what title and name this strange lay

 Branthwaite , our old Eton school friend , a gentleman of as good blood as

 " none well , " cry Fairthorn , who have sidle himself into the group ;

 " permit I to conclude , Mr. Fairthorn , " resume the Colonel ; " Monumental

 have leave not a scrap for the moth . " 

 " ah ! " murmur Lionel , " be it not I , Sophy , who teach you to love your 
 father 's genius ! do you not remember how , as we bent over his volume , it 
 seem to translate to we our own feeling ? - to draw we nearer together ? 
 he be speak to we from his grave . "

 to whom she still clung close and close . 

 " be it so ? be it certain ? be there no doubt that she be the child of 
 these honour parent ? " ask Waife , tremulously . 

 " none , " answer Alban ; " we bring with us proof that will clear up all 
 my story . " 

 the old man bow his head over Sophy 's fair lock for a moment ; then 
 raise it , serene and dignified : " you be mine for a moment yet , Sophy , " 
 say he . 

 " your as ever - more fondly , gratefully than ever , " cry Sophy . 

 " there be but one man to whom I can willingly yield you . Son of Charles 
 Haughton , take my treasure . " 

 " I consent to that , " cry Vance , " though I be put aside like a 
 Remorseless Baron . and , Lionello mio , if Frank Vance be a miser , so much

 " but , " falter Lionel . 

 " oh , falter not . look into those eye ; read that blush now . she look 
 coy , not reluctant . she bend before he - adorn as for love , by 
 all her native grace . Air seem brighten by her bloom . no more the 
 Outlaw - child of Ignominy and Fraud , but the Starry Daughter of POETRY 
 and ART ! Lo , where they glide away under the leafless , melancholy tree . 
 Leafless and melancholy ! no ! Verdure and blossom and the smile of spring 
 be upon every bough ! " 

 " I suppose , " say Alban , " it will not now break Lionel 's heart to learn 
 that not an hour before I leave London , I hear from a friend at the 
 Horse Guards that it have be resolve to substitute the regiment for 
 Lionel 's ; and it will be for some time yet , I suspect , that he must 
 submit to be in gloriously happy . come this way , George , a word in your 
 ear . " and Alban , draw his nephew aside , tell he of Jasper 's state , 
 and of Arabella 's request . " not a word to - day on these mournful topic , 
 to poor Willy . to - day let nothing add to his pain to have lose a 
 grandchild , or dim his consolation in the happiness and security 
 his Sophy gain in that loss . but to - morrow you will go and see the 
 stricken - down sinner , and prepare the father for the bad . I make a

 compare he to a mountain , not merely shatter by an earthquake , but

 " a few week only , " sigh George . " well , Time , that seem everything 
 to man , have not even an existence in the sight of God . to that old man

 train I to kneel by the deathbed of his son ! " 

 " you believe , " ask the Man of the World , " in the efficacy of a

 be go ? " 

 " I believe , " reply the Preacher , " that in health there be nothing 
 so unsafe as trust in a deathbed repentance ; I believe that on the 
 deathbe , it can not be unsafe to repent ! " 

 Alban look thoughtful , and George turn to rejoin Waife , to whom

 impatient to vent in delicate music what he never could have set into 
 word for his blunder , untunable tongue . the Colonel join Darrell , 
 and hasten to unfold more fully the story which Vance be recite to 
 Waife . 

 Brief as it can , be the explanation due to the reader . Vance 's sister 
 have die in childbirth . the poor young poet , unfit to cope with 
 penury , his sensitive nature combine with a frame that could feebly 
 resist the strain of exhaust emotion , disappoint in fame , 
 despairing of fortune , dependent for bread on his wife 's boyish brother , 
 and harass by petty debt in a foreign land , have be fast pin 
 away , even before an affliction to which all the rest seem as nought . 
 with that affliction he break down at once , and die a few day after 
 his wife , leave an infant not a week old . a french female singer , of 
 some repute in the theatre , and make a provincial tour , be lodge 
 in the same house as the young couple . she have that compassionate heart 
 which be more common than prudence or very strict principle with the 
 tribe who desert the prosaic true world for the light sparkle false 
 one . she have assist the young couple , in their later day , with purse 
 and kind office ; have be present at the birth of the infant - the death 
 of the mother ; and have promise Arthur Branthwaite that she would 
 take care of his child , until she could safely convey it to his wife 's 
 relation , while he weep to own that they , poor as himself , must regard 
 such a charge as a burthen . 

 the singer write to apprise Mrs. Vance of the death of her daughter and 
 son - in - law , and the birth of the infant whom she undertake shortly to 
 send to England . but the babe , whom meanwhile she take to herself , get 
 hold of her affection ; with that yearning for child which make 
 so remarkable and almost uniform a characteristic of french woman   if 
 themselves childles   in the wandering bohemian class that separate 
 they from the ordinary household affection , never dead in the heart 
 of woman till womanhood itself be dead , the singer clung to the orphan 
 little one to whom she be for the moment render the care of a 
 mother . she could not bear to part with it ; she resolve to adopt it as 
 her own . the knowledge of Mrs. Vance 's circumstance - the idea that the 
 orphan , to herself a blessing , would be an unwelcome encumbrance to its 
 own relation - remove every scruple from a mind unaccustomed to suffer 
 reflection to stand in the way of an impulse . she write word to Mrs. 
 Vance that the child be dead . she trust that her letter would 
 suffice , without other evidence , to relation so poor , and who could 
 have no suspicion of any interest to deceive they . her trust be 
 well found . Mrs. Vance and the boy Frank , whose full confidence and 
 gratitude have be already secure to their correspondent for her kind 
 office to the young parent , accept , without a demur or a question , 
 the news that the infant be no more . the singer move on to the next 
 town at which she be professionally engage . the infant , hitherto 
 bring up by hand , become ail . the medical adviser call in 
 recommend the natural food , and find , in a village close by , the 
 nurse to whom a little time before Jasper Losely have consign his own 
 daughter . the latter die ; the nurse then remove to Paris to reside 
 with the singer , who have obtain a lucrative appointment at one of the 
 metropolitan theatre . in less than two year the singer herself fall 
 a victim to a prevail epidemic . she have live without thought of the 
 morrow ; her debt exceed her mean ; her effect be sell . the nurse , 
 who have meanwhile become a widow , come for advice and refuge to her 
 sister , who be in the service of Gabrielle Desmarets . Gabrielle , be 
 naturally appeal to , see the infant , hear the story , look into 
 the statement which , by way of confession , the singer have draw up , and 
 sign , in a notary 's presence , before she die ; look into the letter 
 from Mrs. Vance , and the schoolboy scrawl from Frank , both to the 
 singer and to the child 's parent , which the actress have carefully 
 preserve ; convince herself of the poverty and obscurity of the 
 infant 's natural guardian and next of kin ; and say to Jasper , who be 
 just dissipate the fortune hand over to he as survivor of his wife 
 and child : " there be what , if well - manage , may retain your hold on a 
 rich father - in - law when all else have fail . you have but to say 
 that this infant be his grandchild ; the nurse we can easily bribe , 
 or persuade to confirm the tale . I , whom he already know as that 
 respectable baroness , your Matilda 's friend , can give to the story some 
 probable touch . the lone childless man must rejoice to think that 
 a tie be leave to he . the infant be exquisitely pretty ; her face will 
 plead for she . his heart will favour the idea too much to make he very 
 rigorous in his investigation . take the infant . Doubtless in your own 
 country you can find some one to rear it at little or no expense , until 
 the time come for appeal to your father - in - law when no other claim on 
 his purse remain . " 

 Jasper assent with the insouciant docility by which he always 
 acknowledge Gabrielle 's astuter intellect . he see the nurse ; it be 
 clear that she have nothing to gain by take the child to 
 english relation so poor . they might refuse to believe she , and 
 certainly - could not reward . to rid herself of the infant , and obtain 
 the mean to return to her native village with a few hundred franc in 
 her purse , there be no promise she be not willing to make , no story 
 she be too honest to tell , no paper she be too timid to sign . 
 Jasper be go to London on some adventure of his own . he take the 
 infant - chance on Arabella - the reader know the rest . the indifference 
 ever manifest by Jasper to a child not his own - the hardness with 
 which he have contemplate and plan his father 's separation from one 
 whom he have impose by false pretext on the old man 's love , and whom 
 he only regard as an alien encumbrance upon the scanty mean of 
 her delude protector - the fitful and desultory mode in which , when 
  contrary to the reasoning which Gabrielle have base upon a very large 
 experience of the credulity of human nature in general , but in utter 
 ignorance of the nature peculiar to Darrell   his first attempt 
 at imposition have be so scornfully resist by his indignant 
 father - in - law , he have play fast and loose with a means of extortion 
 which , though loth to abandon , he know would not bear any strict 
 investigation ; - all this be now clear to the reader . and the reader will 
 also comprehend why , partly from fear that his father might betray he , 
 partly from a compassionate unwillingness to deprive the old man of a 
 belief in which William Losely say he have find such solace , Jasper , in 
 his last interview with his father , shrink from say , " but she be not 
 your grandchild ! " the idea of recur to the true relation of the 
 child naturally never enter into Jasper 's brain . he consider they to 
 be as poor as himself . they buy from he the child of parent whore they

 Darrell silently return to Alban Morley the paper over which he have 
 cast his eye as they walk slowly to and fro the slop bank of the 
 lake . 

 " it be well , " say he , glance fondly , as Fairthorn have glance before

 its time . " it be well , " he repeat , look away towards that part of

 dread that that poor child should prove to be of my blood , now , in 
 know that she be not , feel a void , a loss ! to Lionel I be so distant 
 a kinsman ! - to his wife , to his child , what can I be ? a rich old

 new - comer in the last form think the head - boy just leave so old ! and

 " I think , " say Alban , plaintively , " that , for a short time at least , 
 I have do with ' painful subject . ' you revel in they ! County Guy , you 
 have not leave school yet ; leave it with credit ; win the good prize . " and 
 Alban plunge at once into the CRISIS . he grow eloquent ; the Party , the 
 Country , the Great Measure to be intrust to Darrell , if he would 
 but undertake it as a member of the Cabinet ; the Peerage , the House of 
 Vipont , and immortal glory ! - eloquent as Ulysses harangue the son of 
 Peleus in Troilus and Cressida . 

 Darrell listen coldly ; only while Alban dwelt on " the measure , " 
 in which , when it be yet too unripe for practical statesman , he have 
 attach his faith as a thinker , the orator 's eye flash with young 
 fire . a great truth be eternally clear to a great heart that have once

 have now wind their walk to the opposite side of the lake , and pause 
 near the thick beech - tree , hallow and sadden by such secret 
 association to the mournful owner . 

 " no , my dear Alban , " say Darrell , " I can not summon up sufficient youth 
 and freshness of spirit to re - enter the turbulent arena I have leave . ah ! 
 look yonder where Lionel and Sophy move ! give I , I do not say Lionel 's 
 year , but Lionel 's wealth of hope , and I might still have a wish for 
 fame and a voice for England ; but it be a subtle truth , that when a man 
 miss a home , a link between his country and himself be go . vulgar 
 ambition may exist - the selfish desire of power ; they be never very 
 strong in I , and now less strong than the desire of rest ; but that

 citizen , which begin at the hearth and widen round the land , be not 
 for the hermit 's cell . " 

 Alban be about to give up the argument in irritable despair , when 
 happen to turn his eye towards the farth depth of the beech - grove ,

 Montfort . restless man , do come near , and stand still . I be tired to 
 death . " Darrell approach , and , lean against the trunk of the giant 
 tree , say , with fold arm and compress lip : 

 " a message from Lady Montfort ! " 

 " yes . I should have tell you , by - the - by , that it be she who , be 
 a woman , of course succeed where I , be a man , despite incredible 
 pain and trouble , signally fail , discover Arabella Fossett , alias 
 Crane , and obtain from she the document which free your life forever 
 from a haunting and torture fear . I urge she to accompany I hither , 
 and place the document herself in your hand . she refuse ; you be not 
 worth so much trouble , my dear Guy . I request she at least to suffer 
 I to show to you a paper contain Jasper Losely 's confession of a 
 conspiracy to poison her mind against you some year ago - a conspiracy 
 so villianously ingenious that it would have completely exonerate any 
 delicate and proud young girl from the charge of fickleness in yield 
 to an impulse of pique and despair . but Lady Montfort do not wish to be 
 exonerate ; your good opinion have cease to be of the slight value to 
 she . but to come to the point . she bid I tell you that , if you persist 
 in shelter yourself in a hermit 's cell from the fear of meet 
 she - if she be so dangerous to your peace - you may dismiss such absurd 
 apprehension . she be go abroad , and between you and I , my dear 
 fellow , I have not a doubt that she will marry again before six month 
 be out . I speak of your suffering ; she tell I she have not the 
 small compassion for they . " 

 " Alban Morley , you presume to talk thus of I ? " cry Darrell , livid 
 with rage . 

 " Strike , but hear I . it be true you would not own , when I be last at 
 Fawley , that she be the cause of your secluded life , of your blighted 
 career ; but I know well . however , let I go on before you strangle 
 I . Lady Montfort 's former feeling of friendship for you be evidently 
 quite change ; and she charge I to add , that she really hop that you 
 would exert your good sense and pride   of which Heaven know you have 
 plenty   to eradicate an absurd and romantic sentiment , so displease to 
 she , and so - " 

 " it be false ! it be false ! what have I do to you , Colonel Morley , that 
 you should slander I thus ? I send you message of taunt and insult , Mr. 
 Darrell ! you can not believe it - you can not ! " 

 Caroline Montfort stand between the two , as if she have drop from 
 heaven . 

 a smile , half in triumph , half in irony , curve the lip of the fine 
 gentleman . it fade instantly as his eye turn from the face of the 
 earnest woman to that of the earnest man . Alban Morley involuntarily 
 bow his head , murmur some word unheard , and pass from the place 
 unheeded . 

 not by concert nor premeditation be Caroline Montfort on that spot ; she 
 have consent to accompany her cousin to Fawley , but before reach the 
 park gate her courage fail she ; she would remain within the carriage ; 
 the Colonel , want in London as soon as possible , whatever the result 
 of his political mission to Darrell , could not stay long at Fawley ; she 
 would return with he . Vance 's presence and impatient desire to embrace 
 his niece do not allow the Colonel an occasion for argument and parley . 
 chafe at this fresh experience of the capricious uncertainty of woman , 
 he have walk on with Vance to the Manor - house . leave alone , Caroline 
 could not endure the stillness and inaction which increase the tumult 
 of her thought ; she would at least have one more look - it might be the

 its first happy dream . but a few yard across those circumscribe

 unperceive in view of the house , the beloved lake , perhaps even once

 on ; she gain the beech - grove , when , by the abrupt wind of the bank ,

 her robe , as she turn to retreat , catch Alban 's eye ; the reader 
 comprehend with what wily intent , conceive on the moment , that 
 unscrupulous schemer shape the word which chain her footstep , and 
 then sting she on to self - disclosure . tremble and blushing , she now

 confuse break word , to explain that she be there but by accident - by 
 chance ; confusion grow deep and deep - how explain the motive that 
 have charm her step to the spot ? 

 suddenly from the opposite bank come the music of the magic flute , and 
 her voice as suddenly stop and fail she . 

 " again - again , " say Darrell , dreamily . " the same music ! the same air ! 
 and this the same place on which we two stand together when I first 
 dare to say , ' I love ! ' look ! we be under the very tree ! look ! there 
 be the date I carve on the bark when you be go , but have leave Hope 
 behind . ah , Caroline , why can I not now resign myself to age ? why be

 to I as a child . I can not - I can not ! go ! " 

 she draw near to he , gently , timidly . " even that , Darrell , - even 
 that ; something in your life - let I be something still ! " 

 " ah , " he say with melancholy bitterness , " you deceive I no long now ! 
 you own that , when here we stand last and exchange our troth , you in

 love , deaf to all calumny , proof to all craft that could wrong the 
 absent ; no woman 's love , warm as the heart , undye as the soul , that

 " Darrell , it be not - though then I think it be . " 

 " ay , ay , " he continue with a smile , as if of triumph in his own pang , 
 ' so that truth be confess at last ! and when , once more free , you write 
 to I the letter I return , rent in fragment , to your hand - or when ,

 anguish I endure in lose you ; but do not tell I that even then you 
 be under the influence of woman 's love . " 

 " Darrell , I be not . " 

 " you own it , and you suffer I to see you again ! trifl and cruel one , 
 be it but to enjoy the sense of your undiminished , unalterable power ? " 

 " alas , Darrell ! alas ! why be I here ? - why so yearning , yet so afraid to 
 come ? why do my heart fail when these tree rise in sight against the 
 sky ? - why , why - why be it draw hither by the spell I could not resist ? 
 Alas , Darrell , alas ! I be a woman now - and - and this - "

 " stay , oh stay ! " cry Darrell . " oh that I could dare to ask you to 
 complete the sentence ! I know - I know by the mysterious sympathy of my 
 own soul , that you could never deceive I more ! be it - be it - " 

 his lip falter too ; but her hand be clasp in his ; her head be

 Fuller and full , spread , wave after wave , throughout the air , till 
 it seem interfuse and commingle with the breath which the listener

 behind that group , shadow by the Thorn - tree , stand the PREACHER , 
 thoughtful and grave , foresee the grief that must come to the old man 
 with the morrow , when he will learn that a guilty son near his end , and 
 will hasten to comfort Jasper 's last day with pardon . but the Preacher 
 look not down to the death - couch alone ; on and high over death look 
 the Preacher ! by what word Heavenly Mercy may lend to his lip shall he 
 steal away , yet in time , to the soul of the dying , and justify murmur 
 of hope to the elm of a life so dark with the shade of its past ? and 
 to he , to the Preacher , they who survive - the two mourner will come 
 in their freshness of sorrow ! he , the old man ? Nay , to he there will be 
 comfort . his spirit Heaven 's kindness have temper to trial ; and , alas

 balm may yet drop on thy wound , thou poor , grim , iron - grey , love 
 woman . 

 Lo ! that traitor , the flute - player , over whom fall the deep grateful 
 shade from the eave of the roof - tree reprieve ; though unconscious as

 and Alban Morley , seemingly intent upon the sketch which his amiable

 and the lake be as smooth as glass ; and the swan , hearken the music ,
